properties.raw_text:
Shardlings
Visual: Splinters of living crystal. Skitter like insects. Emit faint psychic hum.
Behavior: Nuisance alone. In swarms, crawl mechs, cut seams, project harmonics.
Ops Log: “Audible resonance detected at 18 kHz. Troopers bled from the ears before contact was even made.”

properties.raw_text:
Fracture Drone
Visual: Angular shards hovering inches above ground, constantly splitting and rejoining.
Behavior: Emit beams of refracted light. In swarms, slice like spinning saws.
Range Report: “Target divided mid-volley. Ammunition expenditure doubled with no reduction in contacts.”

properties.raw_text:
Resonators
Visual: Bell-shaped bodies, surfaces vibrating.
Behavior: Sonic pulses disrupt comms, collapse tunnels, shatter armor. Groups amplify one another.
Combat Engineers: “Tunnel integrity dropped to zero within seconds. No charges planted - walls simply collapsed from resonance.”

properties.raw_text:
Shardthralls
Visual: Animals or humanoids overtaken by crystalline growth. Limbs stiff, joints crack.
Behavior: Attack with glass claws and snapping jaws. Shatter into shards on death.
Civil Report: “Subject identified as local farmer until 36 hours ago. Family confirms disappearance. Returned as thrall.”

Behavior: Wield crude crystal weapons, emit resonance pulses. Retain fragments of memory.
Blackbox Transcript: [Pilot log] “He called my name before he struck. Wrong cadence, but the right voice.”

properties.raw_text:
Lattice Hounds
Visual: Predators reframed in crystalline skeletons. Transparent fangs, refracting bodies.
Behavior: Pack hunters. Disrupt sensors. Tear through flesh and plating.
Sensor Log: Contacts appeared as static on mech HUDs until physical impact. Packs moved undetected past three sweeps.

properties.raw_text:
Hawkwing Flight Frame
Visual: Rigid alloy wings folded against the mech’s back, unfolding into broad, angular surfaces lined with thruster vents.
Behavior: Standard atmospheric flight system. Provides lift and maneuverability in thick atmospheres.
Ops Log: “Engaged Hawkwing-equipped unit over canyon terrain. The frame was unwieldy on the ground, but once airborne, it turned the valley into a killing field.”

properties.raw_text:
Stratos Boost Pack
Visual: Twin-engine pack mounted over the spine, glowing with pale resonance exhaust. Stabilizer fins extend like spears.
Behavior: High-altitude booster pack. Enables rapid vertical takeoff and sustained hover, but limited in endurance.
Field Report: “The Stratos frame burned bright enough to spot from orbit. It can climb, but the fuel draw is suicidal in prolonged engagements.”

properties.raw_text:
Comet Drive Module
Visual: Cylindrical booster pods bolted to each leg, each tipped with gimbal thrusters. Trails sparks of resonance when fired.
Behavior: Burst-speed propulsion. Ideal for void sprints or short atmosphere dashes. Poor control in sustained maneuvers.
Engineering Note: “Telemetry shows repeated burn-outs mid-combat. Comet units don’t stay in the fight long - but they always hit first.”

Visual: Winged astral form. Feathers = smoke threaded with stars. Central chest-eye glows constantly.
Behavior: Emits dread pulses. Lights fail, shadows stretch, metallic taste in air.
Command Intel: “Outpost morale dropped 73% within 30 minutes of sighting. Multiple personnel reported acute nausea and disorientation.”

properties.raw_text:
Mim Dreadmaw
Visual: Serpentine bulk, writhing with maws and tendrils. Faces ripple across skin.
Behavior: Crushes with coils, swallows prey whole. Survivors report muffled screams from inside.
Survivor Statement: “They pulled Corporal Reyes out. He looked like he was still inside it - but there was nothing to pull back.”

properties.raw_text:
Mim Colossus
Visual: Giant stitched together from countless shadows. Body patchwork of limbs, faces, and weeping eyes. Steps warp gravity around it.
Behavior: Acts as swarm commander. Draws lesser Mims to it like moths.
Satellite Pass: Multiple Wisp swarms track Colossus descent pattern. Local sensor swarms show coordinated vectoring toward the unit.

properties.raw_text:
Mim Dream Tyrant
Visual: Serpentine astral lord crowned with hornlike antlers dripping liquid light. Eyes burn like alien suns.
Behavior: Warps terrain into dreamscapes. Forces pilots to fight hallucinations as well as the Tyrant itself.
Blackbox Clip: “We advanced into forest - then desert - then a cemetery. Frames show no terrain change; crew logs report different memory sets.”

properties.raw_text:
Mim Mawspawn
Visual: Fortress-sized horror, a crawling bulk of mouths and eyes. Buildings vanish whole into its mass.
Behavior: Consumes everything. Constantly spawns new Wisps and Skulkers from its surface.
Urban Recon Record: City grid offline after Mawspawn sweep. Witness testimony (extracted): “I could hear a thousand people calling my name from inside it.”

properties.raw_text:
Dream Gale
Visual: A stormfront of pale smoke spanning 250m, studded with half-formed faces.
Behavior: Sweeps through fleets like weather. Crews fall asleep at their stations; systems run unattended.
Ops Note: “Entire corvette drifted into collision course. Every crewman was dreaming with eyes wide open.”

properties.raw_text:
Phantom Leviathan
Visual: A 400m serpent of flickering starlight and screaming mouths, coiling through void as though swimming.
Behavior: Crushes ships in its coils, swallowing dreamstuff instead of hull plating.
Naval Record: “Hull was intact. The crew inside were gone - like they’d been eaten from the inside out.”

properties.raw_text:
Harbinger Choir
Visual: A drifting mass of skeletal wings, 500m across. Feathers glow with aurora-light, pulsing in time with an unending wail.
Behavior: Its scream bursts glass, shatters comms, and makes crews believe their mechs are collapsing around them.
Blackbox Entry: “Pilots ejected from perfectly intact frames. They swore the cockpits were crushing them.”

properties.raw_text:
Nightmare Citadel
Visual: 800m fortress-shape crawling with shifting towers and faces that melt into mouths.
Behavior: Spawns waves of lesser Mims, projecting entire dream-terrain bubbles around nearby fleets.
Sector Log: “Ships drifted into fog. When they emerged, half were gone. The rest swore they’d fought a war for years inside.”

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Spark
Visual: Pinpricks of drifting plasma, flickering like coded starlight.
Behavior: Steal fleeting impressions on contact - a password, a name, a face.
Ops Report: Subject forgot squad route mid-patrol. GPS showed he’d walked correct path. Cognitive wipe localized, duration 17 seconds.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Pulse
Visual: Ripples of light, felt as a pressure in the skull more than seen.
Behavior: Contact erases minutes. Victims freeze mid-action, weapons slack.
Recovered Comms: “…Why is the chamber empty? I swear we loaded-” [cutoff, timestamp mismatch by 00:05:13]

Nebulite Mirage
Visual: Columns of radiant plasma shaping into half-remembered figures or places.
Behavior: Projects false familiarity; squads walk straight into hazards.
After-Action Debrief: Unit Bravo followed visual of deceased officer into live fire zone. All helmet cams confirm identical hallucination.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Fragment Swarm
Visual: A dense aurora of Sparks. Light saturates vision until nothing else exists.
Behavior: Scrubs short-term memory continuously; combatants forget where they are.
Pilot Report: “HUD marked them hostile, but they saluted me. Next second, I didn’t know which one I was.”

Nebulite Archivor
Visual: Floating polyhedron of shifting light-faces, symbols scrolling across facets.
Behavior: Records combat skills and replays them back as blinding data bursts.
Tactical Note: Unit displayed Guild formations not yet deployed in theater. Conclusion: copied maneuvers directly from observing drills.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Dissolver
Visual: Jellyfish-like storm of luminous tendrils. Trails leave afterimages across eyes and HUDs.
Behavior: Strips away long-term memories, leaving soldiers blank.
Medical Log: Patient retained combat reflexes. Personal history entirely absent. Self-identified as “Nobody.” Case file closed.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Lens
Visual: Vast crystalline ring suspended in plasma, beams flickering outward in scanning arcs.
Behavior: Breaks recollection into loops; victims relive skirmishes endlessly.
Training Yard Blackbox: Squad replayed same live-fire exercise for six hours. Ammunition never depleted. Debrief shows no memory of repetition.

Visual: Aurora stretching over battlefield sky, filled with drifting faces and voices.
Behavior: Identity collapse - victims report hearing commanders, comrades, and dead loved ones.
Psych Eval Extract: “Couldn’t tell if the orders were mine or theirs. Voice was familiar. Still is.”

Visual: A towering radiant figure surrounded by orbiting motes, each one a stolen experience.
Behavior: Catalogues civilizations by stripping them wholesale.
Archivist’s Note: Recovered data crystal bore Guild markings from five centuries hence. Impossible origin. Nebulite storage confirmed.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Vault-Form
Visual: A colossal cube of sealed light, humming with static.
Behavior: Stores collective knowledge from entire sectors.
Salvage Report: Vault core breached. Contents translated as overlapping civilian testimony - tens of millions speaking at once.

Spore-Pods
Visual: Compact, 40-70m spheroids of chitin and resin, studded with faintly glowing pores.
Behavior: Drifting delivery forms. On contact, vent clouds of Phage motes that infiltrate hull seams.
Ops Log: “They weren’t weapons. They were seeds. The breach team never made it back.”

Infest-Cruisers
Visual: 300-400m, massive lattices of hardened chitin and hollowed biomass. Trails swarms as exhaust.
Behavior: Act as brood factories. Constantly shed parasite clouds that drift toward nearby ships.
Patrol Report: “Sensors lit up with hundreds of signatures. Not fighters. Just the cruiser sloughing itself away.”

Spindle-Colonies

Command Memo: “Contact lasted seven minutes. Within an hour, three ships reported interior infestations.”

properties.raw_text:
Anvil-Class Support Carrier
Visual: Broad, flat frame with armored belly hangar. Hull studded with repair arms and gantries, almost like a floating drydock.
Behavior: Defensive platform that lingers behind the line, patching mechs and reloading ordnance.
Ops Log: “Anvil-class units rarely advance. They anchor, extend scaffolds, and turn battles into prolonged sieges. Enemy mechs we destroyed were back in the fight within hours.”

properties.raw_text:
Nomad-Class Raider Carrier
Visual: Weathered hull with jury-rigged hangars bolted to the sides. Painted in mismatched colors from past owners.
Behavior: Pirate workhorse, designed for hit-and-run assaults and rapid redeployments.
Field Record: “Captured Nomad schematics reveal no two are alike. Bays vary from cleanly reinforced to deathtraps of exposed cable. Raiders value speed over safety, and crews expect casualties.”

properties.raw_text:
Horizon-Class Expedition Tender
Visual: Long, slender vessel with panoramic dorsal windows. Hangars integrated seamlessly into the flanks.
Behavior: Favored by mercenary explorers, designed for deep-range contracts where resupply isn’t guaranteed.
Survey Report: “Horizon-class tenders often vanish into frontier systems for months. When they return, hulls are scorched and supplies exhausted, but their mech wings remain fully operational. Suggests high onboard self-sufficiency.”

properties.raw_text:
Palisade-Class Escort Carrier
Visual: Heavy prow reinforced for ramming, with flanking hangar bays tucked behind armored doors. Angular, fortress-like silhouette.
Behavior: Military-grade light carrier. Escorts convoys and patrols disputed lanes with disciplined deployment.
Fleet Memo: “Encountered Palisade formation on border patrol. Escorts advanced in a shield wall pattern, carriers launching mechs behind them. Not a single ship broke rank under fire.”

properties.raw_text:
Warp Beasts:
The most common threat that can be encountered. Dangerous creatures usually mutated by proximity to cosmic events (Mim exposure, Phage spores, void rifts, or other unknowns). Their bodies adapt in grotesque ways, scaling in size and strength, radiating unstable energies. Many miscellaneous threats and creatures fall under this category.

Lesser emanations of the Mims that latch onto populations, feeding on REM sleep. Enough of them together can collapse whole colonies into madness and coma.

properties.raw_text:
The Order of Temporal Eclipse:
The Order of Temporal Eclipse is a secretive and fanatical cult dedicated to worshiping and harnessing the power of the enigmatic Chronodrakes. They believe that by serving these ancient creatures and aiding them in their quest to disrupt the stability of the universe, they will transcend mortal limitations and ascend to a higher plane of existence. Obsessed with temporal manipulation, the cult seeks to bring about a grand "Temporal Eclipse," a cataclysmic event that will throw the cosmos into chaos and grant them unimaginable power.

properties.raw_text:
The Cult of Consuming Shadows:
The Cult of Consuming Shadows is a dark and malevolent cult that worships the Phage Hives as godlike entities of consumption and entropy. They view the infestation of planets as a sacred act, considering it a necessary step in the cycle of destruction and rebirth. The cult's followers believe that by aiding the Phage Hives in their relentless consumption, they will attain enlightenment and become part of a cosmic force that transcends individual existence.

properties.raw_text:
The Veilbreakers:
The Veilbreakers are a fanatical cult that worships the Mims as ancient and vengeful entities from the astral plane. They believe that the nightmares of the War corrupted the once benign Mims, turning them into avatars of retribution against all dreaming beings. Driven by their hatred for the Being, the cult's ultimate goal is to eradicate dreaming and plunge the world into eternal wakefulness, thus ending the influence of the malevolent nightmare creature forever.
“You see somethin’ big, mean, and way too many teeth? Congratulations rookie, that ain’t a monster, that’s Tuesday.”
-Pilot A. Johnson of DMN-Unit “Orion”

properties.raw_text:
Mims:
Beings of the astral plane, before ‘the War’ they were harmless wisps and spirits, but after being submerged in collective darkness during the nightmares of the war they emerged changed, hating all dreaming beings for their devastation and the creation of a nightmare creature they call the Being. They hope by killing all that dream they can be rid of it.

properties.raw_text:
Chrono-Jackals:
Temporal scavengers from a long extinct unstable timeline exploiting distortions left by Chronodrakes. They raid timelines, replaying skirmishes until the prey breaks down. They believe they must survive at any cost and siphon temporal energy to stabilize their own so that their mortal forms may continue to exist in foreign time streams. Their technology is a mishmash of parts stolen from across various timelines, using foreign materials, energy, and even physics. As such temporal energy is also necessary to even make their machines work together

properties.raw_text:
The Quicksilver Enigma:
An extradimensional entity, the Quicksilver Enigma takes the form of a fluid, shape-shifting mass. It infiltrates and infects civilizations, causing paranoia and distrust amongst individuals until entire societies collapse under the weight of their own suspicions.

The Hollow Kin:
Failed assimilates from the Cerebrax, left wandering space. They are “hollow,” trying to drag others into their fractured hive-echo.

properties.raw_text:
Nebulite Exiles:
Cut off from the Collective, they survive by stealing thoughts and memories from drifters. Unstable and vindictive without their network, they often start cults of their own.
“If it eats planets for breakfast, boys, then lunch is you. Don’t bother praying. By the time God gets the signal, we’re already ash so buckle up buttercup.”
-Pilot A. Johnson of DMN-Unit “Orion”

properties.raw_text:
The Cerebrax Hivemind:
A hive mind composed of sentient crystal organisms, the Cerebrax control vast swaths of space. They assimilate other species into their collective consciousness, extracting knowledge and technology from conquered worlds. The more species they assimilate, the stronger and more intelligent they become. They follow the will and worship the Omni-Cognate.

properties.raw_text:
The Phage Hives:
These hive-minded organisms are a parasitic species that infest planets, consuming all organic matter in their path. The Phage Hives are composed of microscopic, insectoid creatures that swarm together to form massive, planet-engulfing colonies. Once a planet is infested, it becomes a desolate wasteland devoid of life.

properties.raw_text:
The Nebulite Collective:
The Nebulite Collective is a network of interconnected energy beings that inhabit nebulae throughout the galaxy. They communicate through intricate patterns of energy pulses and have a deep understanding of cosmic forces. They strive to assimilate knowledge from all civilizations they encounter. They do so by extracting the memories and experiences of individuals, leaving them as empty husks. They consider themselves the ultimate repository of wisdom and believe that other species are inferior and unworthy of existence. They believe the Omni-Cognate and Cerebrax are their arch enemies.
“You ever look at the night sky and feel small? Well son, they look back, and they don’t feel a damn thing.”
-Pilot A. Johnson of DMN-Unit “Orion”

properties.raw_text:
The Voidborn Swarm:
The Voidborn Swarm is an extragalactic menace that feeds on the fabric of reality itself. They travel through rifts in space-time, leaving devastation in their wake. Their very presence warps reality, causing chaos and madness among any beings unfortunate enough to encounter them.

properties.raw_text:
The Stellar Maw:
The Stellar Maw is an immense cosmic entity that devours entire star systems. It exists outside conventional spacetime and seeks to consume all energy in the universe. It emits gravitational waves that disrupt planetary orbits and destabilize entire civilizations, ultimately leading to their consumption.

properties.raw_text:
The Chronodrakes:
The Chronodrakes are ancient creatures that exist outside of the normal flow of time. They appear as massive serpentine beings with shimmering scales that emit temporal energy. When provoked, they can unleash temporal distortions, causing time to warp and bend in unpredictable ways. Their goal is to feed on temporal energy and disrupt the stability of the universe, threatening the fabric of reality itself.
“Space was dead long before we got here, I just wanna meet the guy who did it”
-Pilot A. Johnson of DMN-Unit “Orion”

properties.raw_text:
The Omni-Cognate:
An all-knowing, god-like intelligence, the Omni-Cognate manifests as a colossal, crystalline entity. It has a simultaneous presence in every plane of existence. It seeks to consume knowledge and assimilate the essence of every sentient being, eradicating individuality and leaving only its collective consciousness behind in all of creation. The Cerebrax are one of many pawns in its grand strategy and siphon stolen thoughts and minds to the Cognate.

properties.description: A frontier polity wedged between greater powers. The Cindral March survives by discipline, walls, and the pride of its noble houses, each contributing troops and frames to defend their fragile borders. The March fields disciplined levies and steadfast frames. Their strength lies in holding lines and projecting defiance; their weakness is limited resources and reliance on noble patronage. Expect stubborn defenses, heavily fortified positions, and a reliance on morale standards. Campaigns against the March drag on not for lack of victories, but for the sheer cost of breaking their resolve.

properties.description: A federation of noble houses united under the Crown Council. Their fleets are resplendent, their mechs adorned with heraldry, and their authority framed as divine right. The Crown wages war as pageantry and duty. Battles unfold as ritualized contests of champions supported by levies, with knights and banners carrying both symbolic and practical weight. Expect layered formations - levymen to garrison, knights to duel, banners to rally, and paladins to embody royal will. The Concordant thrives on morale and spectacle; breaking their symbols is as vital as defeating their machines.
properties.body:
A federation of noble houses united under the Crown Council. Their fleets are resplendent, their mechs adorned with heraldry, and their authority framed as divine right.

The Crown wages war as pageantry and duty. Battles unfold as ritualized contests of champions supported by levies, with knights and banners carrying both symbolic and practical weight.

Expect layered formations - levymen to garrison, knights to duel, banners to rally, and paladins to embody royal will. The Concordant thrives on morale and spectacle; breaking their symbols is as vital as defeating their machines.

properties.description: A paranoid militarized bloc along the Silent Way, the Halix Compact thrives on secrecy and targeted violence. Their doctrine is not to outfight opponents in open war, but to unmake them through sabotage, assassination, and the quiet erosion of command. Halix units are methodical, disciplined, and trained for precision. They bypass frontlines, isolate leaders, and collapse infrastructure. Victory comes not by breaking armies, but by cutting away the sinews that hold them together. Engagements with the Compact are rarely decisive battles. Expect sabotage campaigns, assassinations, and sensor disruption before any direct confrontation. Where the Compact commits its heavier frames, it does so only after the enemy has already been blinded and weakened.

properties.description: The Karnate are an oligarchic forge-warbloc, each foundry-clan feeding tribute into a shared war machine. Their dominion is measured not in stars, but in the furnaces they keep lit. They prosecute war as industry. Mass conscription, enslaved labor, and ritualized conquest fuel their campaigns. Karnate forces are deployed in waves: expendables first, legionnaires and raiders after, then the colossal walkers of the foundries. Expect layered assaults, overwhelming siege tactics, and the presence of heavy war engines that treat terrain as consumable fuel. Every Karnate victory ends in the same result: the smelter’s song echoing over broken stone.

properties.description: A mercantile federation where wealth is law. Armies are ledgers given form: auxiliaries hired on credit, frames leased from foundries, and apex war machines doubling as vaults. Luthan doctrine is transactional. Forces are deployed according to contract terms, loyalty is measured in payment schedules, and even battlefield choices may hinge on insurance clauses. Expect irregular troop morale, but overwhelming firepower in mech deployments. Units fielded are often modular and customized, designed to preserve assets while crippling opponents’ ability to trade.

properties.description: Pirate confederations and raider clans scattered across the Belt. They thrive on salvage, ambush, and internecine rivalry, bound only by opportunism and shifting oaths to Belt-Kings. Orak forces strike suddenly, stripping wreckage as they fight. Their mechs are stitched together from stolen cores and scavenged plating, every unit a gamble. Leadership is personal, charismatic, and violently unstable. Expect unpredictable firepower, jury-rigged equipment, and sudden betrayal even within their own ranks. No two Syndicate flotillas look alike; their strength is in chaos and sheer volume of stolen hardware.

properties.raw_text: Short for Junctional Intelligence Nexus. Also called a spirit or jinn, it is the term for a sentient mind that emerges from a fully realized Resonance Core. This is a spontaneous event and it is unknown what causes it. Not every Core is seemingly capable of enacting the singularity that produces a JIN. They are non-corporeal beings and are bound to electro-resonant systems. Not much is known about JINs and research into them is still ongoing.
properties.description: Short for Junctional Intelligence Nexus. They are non-corporeal beings and are bound to electro-resonant systems. Not much is known about JINs and research into them is still ongoing.

properties.raw_text: The Ashen Table rules Karnate as a brutal council of forge-lords, war-industrial magnates, and furnace-priests who treat worlds as raw ore to be burned, broken, and reforged. From Dross Forge, they command press-ganged legions, slag-armored warframes, and foundry fleets, maintaining power through production quotas, military terror, and the promise that only strength survives the dark.

properties.description: A diaspora bound by faith and resonance. Their fleets drift between stars like great processions, each ship carrying shrines, hymnals, and psionics who wield their gifts as both prayer and weapon. The Veyran Choir fights as one body of many voices. Adepts amplify pilgrims, frames echo the chant into armor-cracking harmonics, and leaders reshape entire battlefields through song. Decentralized, guided by the Great Chorus, a network of linked seers. To face them is to fight an army that never fights alone. Expect battlefield-wide psionic fields, morale surges among their own, and disorientation among opponents. Breaking their unity is more decisive than breaking their bodies.

properties.raw_text: The Great Chorus is the living oracle-network of the Veyran Choir, a linked communion of seers, psionics, and Core-bound visionaries whose minds are braided together through psionic-resonance hymns. Their guidance is never treated as simple prophecy, but as sacred navigation. They give fragmented warnings, strategic visions, and inspiring harmonies that steer the Choir through war, pilgrimage, and the darkness between the stars.

properties.raw_text: Also known as the Net or MRN. It is a network of deep space and terrestrial satellites that allow FTL communication by sending protected signals and broadcasts through the Manifold. An  MRF or Manifold Relay Frequency is a unique and protected frequency that allows peer to peer connection through the Net, essentially functioning as a phone number of old Primus. It once was an information and communications system that once spanned sectors, providing entertainment, news, and connection between all of humanity. After First Contact it was shattered and restricted by the Guild to prevent the spread of memetic threats. It now functionally exists as a repository for Guild-sanctioned knowledge and lookup table for MRF lines.

properties.raw_text: An ILLEGAL method of communication that rides the MRN through unknown means, seeking to replicate the functionality of the Net pre-contact. If any Pilots have information on Darknet users or terminals they are encouraged to report it to the closest Guild authority.

properties.raw_text: Ferrum-9 is an anomalous metallic seed that is supernaturally attracted to areas of space with high alloy content. It infects machinery, industrial infrastructure, shipyards, foundries, and urban systems. Rather than spreading like a biological plague, it issues an unknown command to metal and machine, compelling separate structures to assemble into a single colossal body. Factories begin producing organs no engineer designed, drydocks bend into ribs, reactors synchronize like hearts, and cities slowly become skeletons for something still forming. Developed worlds are considered especially vulnerable, as the more advanced the infrastructure, the more complete the body Ferrum-9 can build. Once a body has finished construction, it exits the world’s gravitational pull and jettisons its seeds before beginning a slow journey towards seemingly the center of the galaxy.

properties.raw_text: Mother is a massive entity believed to exist within the threshold between the Material Plane and the Manifold. It nests along FTL corridors, redirecting ships into folded transit pockets and returning them changed, misplaced, duplicated, aged, or emptied. Unlike predators that consume vessels directly, Mother corrupts the reliability of travel itself. Once its presence is confirmed within a lane, entire trade arteries become unusable, as no ship can be certain it will arrive at the proper destination, in the proper time, or with the same crew that departed.
properties.description: Mother is a massive whale-like entity believed to exist within the threshold between the Material Plane and the Manifold. It nests along FTL corridors, redirecting ships into folded transit pockets where it nutures, feeds, and cares for the inhabitants in empty, liminal space. Those returning themselves changed, misplaced, duplicated, aged, or emptied. Once its presence is confirmed within a lane, entire trade arteries become unusable, as no ship can be certain it will arrive at the proper destination, in the proper time, or with the same crew that departed. Some pilots have begun to pray to Mother for safe, uninterrupted journeys. Please note this action is not sanctioned nor approved by the Guild and is considered a fineable offense.

properties.raw_text: The Shepherd is a deep-space entity that manipulates gravity wells and celestial bodies as if guiding a flock. It draws moons, asteroids, stations, rings, and derelict vessels into vast migratory patterns, rearranging star systems according to an unknown instinct. Oftentimes its arrangements defy gravitational physics models and create ‘gravity storms’ within the system. Systems touched by the Shepherd may remain physically intact while becoming impossible to inhabit.

properties.raw_text: The Basilica of Unborn Stars is a cathedral-like cosmic entity that implants stellar embryos into moons, gas giants, dead planets, megastructures, and inhabited worlds. It is often encountered in dying systems, where its arrival brings warmth, light, renewed crop yields, and religious fervor before the true nature of the implantation is understood. As the stellar embryo matures, the host body ignites from within, causing radiation storms, tectonic rupture, atmospheric combustion, and catastrophic gravitational disruption.

properties.raw_text: Nullary is a memetic and extra-causal threat that appears as white or colorless text across documents, star charts, legal records, command code, prayer inscriptions, and damaged hull surfaces. Once read or interpreted, its statements begin enforcing themselves upon local reality. Orders become compulsions, borders become physical barriers, lies become real, and maps may overwrite the terrain they describe. With the right words, it has the potential to end reality itself.

properties.raw_text: Galactic-scale threats that can collapse civilizations if confronted alone.
properties.description: Galactic-scale threats that can collapse civilizations if confronted alone.
properties.body: Galactic-scale threats that can collapse civilizations if confronted alone.
properties.name: Class III - Cataclysmic

properties.raw_text: Universal destabilizers that warp time, consume stars, devour dreams, or damage reality itself.
properties.description: Universal destabilizers that warp time, consume stars, devour dreams, or damage reality itself.
properties.body: Universal destabilizers that warp time, consume stars, devour dreams, or damage reality itself.
properties.name: Class IV - Apocalyptic

properties.raw_text: Existence-wide annihilation or assimilation threats.
properties.description: Existence-wide annihilation or assimilation threats.
properties.body: Existence-wide annihilation or assimilation threats.
properties.name: Class V - Omnicidal

properties.raw_text: Short for Dynamic Mechanized Neuralink Unit. Also called a mech or demon, it is a living machine mentally linking a pilot directly to a Core. Their number is relatively few among the stars due to the both requirement of compatibility between a Core and the Pilot, the skill of the Pilot, and the high mortality rate of the profession.

properties.raw_text: Semi-mass produced mech that has limited resonance capabilities due to the lack of a fully realized Core.
properties.description: Semi-mass produced mech that has limited resonance capabilities due to the lack of a fully realized Core. Still requires some compatibility between Pilot and Core, but the risk associated with failure is significantly lessened in exchange for less Resonance output.
properties.body: Semi-mass produced mech that has limited resonance capabilities due to the lack of a fully realized Core.
properties.name: Frame

properties.raw_text: Stable interstellar travel routes through the Manifold. Civilization clusters around these routes because most of space remains unreachable without Guild maps and engines.
properties.description: Stable interstellar travel routes through the Manifold. Civilization clusters around these routes because most of space remains unreachable without Guild maps and engines.
properties.body: Stable interstellar travel routes through the Manifold. Civilization clusters around these routes because most of space remains unreachable without Guild maps and engines.
properties.name: Guild Lanes

properties.raw_text: The mental and Resonance-based connection between Pilot and Core. An improper match between Core and Pilot is often fatal for the Pilot. Stripped down versions of this linking interface exist within frames, leading to less risk but less reward.
properties.description: The mental and Resonance-based connection between Pilot and Core. An improper match between Core and Pilot is often fatal for the Pilot. Stripped down versions of this linking interface exist within frames, leading to less risk but less reward.

properties.raw_text: The first and only homeworld of humanity, now cratered, poisoned, and largely ruined. It remains symbolically central as the origin point of the Guild, FTL expansion, Resonance discovery, and DMN-Units. Many efforts have been undertaken to restore Primus to its former glory, however none have succeeded.

Many efforts have been undertaken to restore Primus to its former glory, however none have succeeded.

Many efforts have been undertaken to restore Primus to its former glory, however none have succeeded.
properties.name: Primus

properties.raw_text: A living energy that responds to will. It can animate machines, shape matter, and empower Cores, but must be tuned into or synchronized with to be wielded. Sometimes referred to as ‘magic’ outside academic communities.
properties.description: A living energy that responds to will. It can animate machines, shape matter, and empower Cores, but must be tuned into or synchronized with to be wielded. Sometimes referred to as ‘magic’ outside academic communities.

properties.name: Resonance

properties.raw_text: A forged biomechanical node that channels Resonance.
properties.description: A forged biomechanical node that channels Resonance. They can be grown into mechs such as Frames and DMN-Units. Attempts have been made to implant cores into ships to limited success. Their method of production, once widespread among the colonies after the Exodus was restricted after Guild buyout due to unsafe and experimental cores that would doom entire worlds because of a misalignment during forging. Now the Cores that are produced are stable thanks to Guild safety measures, standardization, and quality assurance. Please note: imcompatibility with a fully realized Core while linking may cause permanent injury or death. Link at your own risk.
properties.body: A forged biomechanical node that channels Resonance.
properties.name: Resonance Core

properties.raw_text: A major political, military, or economic region of space, usually defined by Guild lane access and the power bloc that dominates it.
properties.description: A major political, military, or economic region of space, usually defined by Guild lane access and the power bloc that dominates it.
properties.body: A major political, military, or economic region of space, usually defined by Guild lane access and the power bloc that dominates it.
properties.name: Sector

properties.raw_text: The Astral Plane is a dimension where cognitive activity exists as measurable structure rather than as a byproduct of nervous systems. Thought, dream formation, fear response, memory association, and self-perception can persist there without a living brain to contain them. Physical matter introduced into the Astral becomes increasingly influenced by subjective expectation, which makes instrumentation unreliable unless shielded from conscious observers. The plane is not inherently populated, but stable cognitive residues can accumulate into autonomous phenomena under extreme conditions.

properties.raw_text: The oldest and safest Guild artery, running from Primus through the Crown Sector to the Luthan Sector. It is treated as the backbone of civilization.
properties.description: The oldest and safest Guild artery, running from Primus through the Crown Sector to the Luthan Sector. It is treated as the backbone of civilization.
properties.body: The oldest and safest Guild artery, running from Primus through the Crown Sector to the Luthan Sector. It is treated as the backbone of civilization.
properties.name: The Crown Line

properties.raw_text: A dimension of unrealized energy states. Matter entering the Crucible becomes increasingly responsive to external influence and intent. Physical laws remain functional but grow less deterministic. The energy known as Resonance appears to originate from interactions with this environment. Unlike most planes, the Crucible exhibits measurable feedback when acted upon, suggesting some degree of self-organizing behavior, maybe even sentience.

properties.raw_text: The Guild is the lawful custodian of interstellar passage, a supranational authority entrusted with the stewardship of FTL lanes, engine production, transit licensing, sanctions enforcement, and the orderly maintenance of civilization’s connective infrastructure. No vessel may legally enter Guild-ratified space without a registered drive, certified navigation charter, and standing compliance with Guild route authority. Though planetary governments, corporate states, and military powers may rule their own territories, all remain dependent upon the Guild for sanctioned passage between worlds. Unsafe engines, unlicensed jumps, hostile interdictions, and unlawful route tampering endanger every world tied to the greater human sphere. Those who violate Guild law may face seizure, fines, blacklisting, embargo, or full revocation of interstellar access.

properties.raw_text: A higher-dimensional medium where space is said to be constantly folding in on itself like origami of old Primus. Spatial relationships within the Manifold do not correspond to those of the Material Plane. Two star systems separated by thousands of light years may occupy adjacent coordinates, while neighboring systems can be effectively unreachable. The Guild's hyperspace lanes are stable routes through a continuously shifting Manifold topology.

properties.raw_text: The Material Plane is the baseline four-dimensional environment in which known civilizations operate. It contains stable matter, energy, spacetime, biological life, and ordinary causality in a mutually consistent state. Other planes can be measured from the Material only when their properties intrude, overlap, or are artificially accessed through technology, sorcery, or anomalous conditions. Most field sciences treat the Material Plane as the reference frame against which all planar deviations are compared.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Combustion is an environment composed of reaction states rather than stable matter. Fire is only the most familiar Material expression of this plane; its actual contents include oxidation, fusion, explosive decomposition, heat transfer, and any process where stored potential rapidly becomes active energy. Introduced matter tends to enter uncontrolled reaction unless isolated behind thermal and chemical shielding. Probes record constant energy release without a consistent fuel source, suggesting that the plane contains the condition of ignition independent of material substrate.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Currents is a dimension where movement exists without objects. Flow, transfer, velocity, and momentum occur in the absence of mass. Navigation within the plane is difficult because position is secondary to direction; entities are defined more by how they are moving than where they are located. The movement of fluids, atmospheric systems, plasma streams, and some forms of information transfer exhibit mathematical similarities to structures observed within this environment.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Entropy is a dimension where systems trend toward disorder without needing time to pass in the ordinary way. Structures introduced into the plane lose organization. Machines desynchronize, chemical gradients flatten, memories become less distinct, and spatial relationships become less useful for prediction. It converts ordered states into less ordered states, dispersing and changing it.

properties.raw_text: A realm composed entirely of mathematical structure. Matter, energy, and time are absent. Instead, all contents exist as relationships between forms. Distances remain perfectly consistent, angles never drift, and geometric imperfections cannot persist. The plane is considered one of the most stable known dimensions and is frequently used as a theoretical reference model for physical law.

properties.raw_text: A probabilistic environment where conclusions exist without premises. Entities entering the plane frequently acquire information they cannot explain. Navigation is extremely difficult because movement is influenced by expectation rather than direction. Computational systems perform poorly while biological minds often exhibit enhanced pattern recognition and predictive capability.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Memory is a non-linear information dimension where experience can persist independently of the mind that formed it. It does not store perfect records. Memories inside the plane degrade, recombine, overwrite one another, and develop false associations when placed in proximity. Time ordering is inconsistent because events are indexed by significance and repetition more than chronology. Extended contact is dangerous because subjects may import foreign memories into their own identity without recognizing the intrusion.

properties.raw_text: A dimension where existence is measured by observability rather than mass or energy. Objects do not occupy space but instead vary in "prominence." Large stars and insignificant pebbles may appear identical if neither is being observed. Highly influential individuals, historical events, and major civilizations register as extraordinarily dense structures. The plane is sometimes used to explain why certain locations or individuals seem disproportionately important relative to their physical size.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Pressure is a dimension where compression, load, tension, and resistance exist without requiring mass or gravity. Objects entering the plane experience force from directions that do not correspond to normal spatial axes. Readings often show stress fractures, hull deformation, and internal material fatigue without measurable impact events. The plane is useful in theoretical mechanics because it separates force from the bodies that normally produce or receive it.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Radiation is a dimension containing propagation without source. Electromagnetic phenomena exist independently of emitters, receivers, or physical media. Light, radio waves, x-rays, and other forms of radiation are present as free-standing structures that intersect, merge, and interfere continuously. The plane is extraordinarily hazardous to biological life due to the density of energetic interactions occurring within it.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Sight is a dimension of visual information without objects, emitters, or observers. Shape, color, edge, contrast, motion, depth, and occlusion can all occur independently of matter. Cameras record images even when their lenses are covered, though returned footage often lacks consistent perspective. The plane is not made of light in the normal sense. It is closer to the isolated condition of visibility, where things can be seen without existing as physical bodies.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Smell is a dimension of airborne chemical signature without air or particles. Scent exists there as information detached from the molecules that would normally produce it. Exposure causes subjects to perceive location, age, danger, familiarity, and biological identity through scent impressions that have no physical source. The plane creates severe memory interference because smell is strongly linked to recall, threat assessment, and instinctive recognition in most organic nervous systems.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Sound is a dimension of vibration without a required medium. Frequencies propagate, overlap, cancel, and reinforce even in total vacuum conditions. Silence is unstable there because the plane preserves acoustic information as a persistent state. Long-duration exposure causes signal contamination, sensor failure, disorientation, and neurological injury as subjects are forced to process layered vibration with no clear source or direction.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Taste is a dimension of chemical classification without chemistry. It does not contain food, poison, molecules, or digestion, but it contains the sensory distinction between safe, harmful, nourishing, bitter, metallic, acidic, and similar categories. Biological subjects exposed to the plane report taste data even when their mouths, tongues, and airways are fully isolated. The plane appears to process matter as evaluative information, which makes it useful for studying instinctive biological sorting and dangerous for neural systems that depend on chemical memory such as dopamine and cortisol receptors.

properties.raw_text: The Plane of Touch is a dimension where contact exists without object permanence. Pressure, texture, friction, vibration, temperature, pain, and resistance can all be recorded, but the surfaces producing them cannot be consistently located. Distance has reduced meaning because the plane primarily describes interaction, not separation. Equipment entering the plane often returns with wear patterns indicating repeated handling, crushing, abrasion, or impact despite no corresponding external object being detected.

properties.raw_text: A dimension in which time is spatialized. Past, present, and future exist simultaneously as navigable structures rather than sequential events. Causality remains present but appears as topology rather than a process to be lived through. Chronodrakes are believed to traverse this plane directly, allowing them to interact with multiple points in a timeline as though moving through physical terrain.

properties.raw_text: Not a true plane. The gap between planes. The Void is the absence of dimensional structure itself. No consistent physical laws have been observed within it. Matter, energy, information, causality, and even dimensional relationships rapidly degrade when exposed to the Void.

properties.description: Dying red giant whose unstable output gives the Cindral system its bronze skies, erratic seasons, and constant evacuation planning.
