Ardane System
Sector Name: Crown Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Ardane is the Crown council capital, a temperate world of palace-cities, embassy keeps, dueling grounds, and noble estates. Guild pilots should observe house protocol carefully, since a minor insult here can become a sector deployment.
properties.description: The Ardane system surrounds the Crown’s council world with the mines of Orren, the reservoir seas of Mereveil, the proving grounds of Valecourt, the reserve holdings of Thorne, and the patrol anchorages around Gannet. Palace-cities and embassy keeps dominate Ardane itself, but policy made there depends on an entire network of noble houses and ceremonial tournament grounds.
properties.body: Ardane is the Crown council capital, a temperate world of palace-cities, embassy keeps, dueling grounds, and noble estates. Guild pilots should observe house protocol carefully, since a minor insult here can become a sector deployment.
properties.tags_text: Ardane System Crown Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Ardane System

properties.raw_text:
Velbrant System
Sector Name: Crown Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Velbrant is a fortress world and tourney system, used to train, test, and display Crown military prestige. Licensed observers are permitted during formal contests, but active interference in knightly rites is grounds for immediate sanction.
properties.description: Velbrant is a binary fortress system whose bright primary and red companion illuminate tourney grounds, metal worlds, gun moons, escort yards, and the Whitefire early-warning array. The Grand Lists and High Marshal’s Ring make the system a stage for Crown prestige, but its distributed defenses and outer fortress moon ensure that every ceremony can become a mobilization.
properties.body: Velbrant is a fortress world and tourney system, used to train, test, and display Crown military prestige. Licensed observers are permitted during formal contests, but active interference in knightly rites is grounds for immediate sanction.
properties.tags_text: Velbrant System Crown Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Velbrant System

properties.raw_text:
Hallowmere System
Sector Name: Crown Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Hallowmere is the Crown’s agricultural heart, a breadbasket world of gene-crop plains, reservoir seas, orchard continents, and cathedral-granaries. Dream-Eater alerts are common enough that pilots should treat rural quiet as a potential warning sign.
properties.description: The Hallowmere system is the Crown’s agricultural heart, combining the inhabited breadbasket world with Orchard Crown, Ceresin’s managed seas, reservoir moons, seed vaults, grain reserves, and industrial support on Vellum. Dream-Eater alerts across its farms, moons, and storage routes are common enough that pilots should treat sudden silence anywhere in the system as a warning.
properties.body: Hallowmere is the Crown’s agricultural heart, a breadbasket world of gene-crop plains, reservoir seas, orchard continents, and cathedral-granaries. Dream-Eater alerts are common enough that pilots should treat rural quiet as a potential warning sign.
properties.tags_text: Hallowmere System Crown Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Hallowmere System

properties.raw_text:
Kaelith System
Sector Name: Crown Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Kaelith is a border crown system of fortified moon-cities, pirate-burned settlements, and anxious noble keeps. It serves as the Concordant’s warning bell on the rough edge of civilized space.
properties.description: Kaelith is a red-dwarf border system built around the gas giant Avarn, its inhabited moon, the mining world Skarn, and the paired Bracken and Hest redoubts.
properties.body: Kaelith is a border crown system of fortified moon-cities, pirate-burned settlements, and anxious noble keeps. It serves as the Concordant’s warning bell on the rough edge of civilized space.
properties.tags_text: Kaelith System Crown Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Kaelith System

properties.raw_text:
Braelyn Hold System
Sector Name: Althoren Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Braelyn Hold is a castle-fortress planet whose mountains, valleys, roads, and orbitals are all designed with defense in mind. Pilots assigned here should expect ceremonial hospitality overlaid with active invasion planning.
properties.description: The Braelyn Hold system is arranged as a layered fortress, from the industrial world Braelyn Hold and its gun moon to Shieldward, Vault Moon, the Iron Halo called the Herald Glass, and the outer anchor at Banner’s Rest. Ceremonial hospitality masks a defense plan written across orbital firing corridors.
properties.body: Braelyn Hold is a castle-fortress planet whose mountains, valleys, roads, and orbitals are all designed with defense in mind. Pilots assigned here should expect ceremonial hospitality overlaid with active invasion planning.
properties.tags_text: Braelyn Hold System Althoren Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Braelyn Hold System

properties.raw_text:
Kether Vale System
Sector Name: Althoren Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Kether Vale is an agrarian borderland of river continents, grazing basins, scattered baronies, and thin defenses. It feeds frontier fleets and absorbs Crown expansion pressure, but response times from higher authority are unreliable.
properties.description: Kether Vale is an agrarian frontier system of river continents, water worlds, scattered baronies, cold outer planets, and lightly defended moons. Its farms and herds feed Crown fleets, but the distance between Kether Vale, Low Vale, Rill, and the outer holdings leaves local lords dependent on slow convoys and unreliable reinforcement.
properties.body: Kether Vale is an agrarian borderland of river continents, grazing basins, scattered baronies, and thin defenses. It feeds frontier fleets and absorbs Crown expansion pressure, but response times from higher authority are unreliable.
properties.tags_text: Kether Vale System Althoren Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Kether Vale System

properties.raw_text:
High Alder Span System
Sector Name: Althoren Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: High Alder Span is a lane-bridge watch built around a Guild-compatible transit array stretched between gravitational anchor points. Its maintenance clans and customs knights keep the rear Crown loop connected and monitor unauthorized movement.
properties.description: High Alder Span is a binary lane-bridge system whose Guild-compatible transit array links Aldergate, Spanhold, Far Alder, and their watch moons across a difficult gravitational corridor. Maintenance clans and customs knights live throughout the system, keeping the rear Crown loop open while monitoring traffic that attempts to bypass the recognized span.
properties.body: High Alder Span is a lane-bridge watch built around a Guild-compatible transit array stretched between gravitational anchor points. Its maintenance clans and customs knights keep the rear Crown loop connected and monitor unauthorized movement.
properties.tags_text: High Alder Span System Althoren Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: High Alder Span System

properties.raw_text:
Duris System
Sector Name: Seldran Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Duris is a ruined throne-world orbiting a white dwarf, preserved as both wound and warning. Exiled houses, salvage crews, and political archaeologists still search its broken palaces for proof of old claims.
properties.description: Duris is a ruined white-dwarf system where the broken throne-world, Mausoleum Moon, Ashen Testament, salvage cradles, and the Archive of Claims preserve the wreckage of a fallen royal order. Exiled houses, scavengers, and political archaeologists search across its palaces, docks, and debris fields for evidence capable of reviving old claims.
properties.body: Duris is a ruined throne-world orbiting a white dwarf, preserved as both wound and warning. Exiled houses, salvage crews, and political archaeologists still search its broken palaces for proof of old claims.
properties.tags_text: Duris System Seldran Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Duris System

properties.raw_text:
Marnholt System
Sector Name: Seldran Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Marnholt is a contested claimant world where rival banners divide continents, orbital platforms, and noble fleets. Guild personnel should avoid endorsing local legitimacy claims unless operating under explicit Mandate instruction.
properties.description: Marnholt is a contested claimant system in which rival banners divide the inhabited world, its moon, the Blue and Gold orbital walls, Palevine, Oathscar, and competing fleet anchorages around Herald. Guild personnel should avoid treating any single capital, platform, or noble fleet as the uncontested authority unless operating under explicit Mandate instruction.
properties.body: Marnholt is a contested claimant world where rival banners divide continents, orbital platforms, and noble fleets. Guild personnel should avoid endorsing local legitimacy claims unless operating under explicit Mandate instruction.
properties.tags_text: Marnholt System Seldran Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Marnholt System

properties.raw_text:
Sable Crown System
Sector Name: Seldran Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Sable Crown is an exile court and intelligence hub sheltered in the radiation shadow of a neutron star. It is formally a political refuge, but in practice it functions as a market for secrets, claimants, and revenge.
properties.description: Sable Crown is an exile network hidden within the radiation geometry of a neutron star, centered on Mourning and a cluster of silent stations, signal-locked depots, caches, and concealed arsenals. The system is formally a refuge for displaced claimants, but its distributed court and storage sites function as a market for secrets, leverage, and revenge.
properties.body: Sable Crown is an exile court and intelligence hub sheltered in the radiation shadow of a neutron star. It is formally a political refuge, but in practice it functions as a market for secrets, claimants, and revenge.
properties.tags_text: Sable Crown System Seldran Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Sable Crown System

properties.raw_text:
Bastrel Gate System
Sector Name: Bastrel Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Bastrel Gate is the Concordant’s expansion-zone staging starport, packed with barracks orbitals, troop elevators, warehouse moons, and permanent temporary camps. Pilots should expect heavy traffic, shifting orders, and competing noble chains of command.
properties.description: Bastrel Gate is an expansion-zone staging system spread across Muster, Pilgrim’s Rest, Garrison, the gas giant Quartermaster, its warehouse moons, and the central transit hub. Barracks orbitals, troop elevators, supply depots, and temporary camps keep the entire system in constant motion under several competing noble chains of command.
properties.body: Bastrel Gate is the Concordant’s expansion-zone staging starport, packed with barracks orbitals, troop elevators, warehouse moons, and permanent temporary camps. Pilots should expect heavy traffic, shifting orders, and competing noble chains of command.
properties.tags_text: Bastrel Gate System Bastrel Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Bastrel Gate System

properties.raw_text:
Stonereach System
Sector Name: Bastrel Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Stonereach is a cold, iron-rich military foundry system run through noble-owned manufactories. It supplies Crown knight frames with armor, lances, and replacement parts, making labor unrest here a strategic risk.
properties.description: Stonereach is a cold military foundry system whose red dwarf supports the metal worlds Stonereach and Ferric, their mining moons, the reserve world Blackslag, and fuel operations around Cinderball. Noble manufactories across the system produce armor, lances, and replacement parts for Crown knight frames, making unrest on any major extraction route strategically dangerous.
properties.body: Stonereach is a cold, iron-rich military foundry system run through noble-owned manufactories. It supplies Crown knight frames with armor, lances, and replacement parts, making labor unrest here a strategic risk.
properties.tags_text: Stonereach System Bastrel Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Stonereach System

properties.raw_text:
Veld Spire System
Sector Name: Bastrel Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Veld Spire is a pulsar-based early warning system of shielded towers, observation platforms, and high-sensitivity signal stations. Its crews watch for pirates, Warp Beasts, lane anomalies, and unauthorized fleet movement.
properties.description: Veld Spire is a pulsar-based warning system consisting of the shielded array over Veld, the outer world Pulseward, and the Zenith, Meridian, and Nadir observatories. Its separated platforms compare signals across the entire system to detect pirates, Warp Beasts, lane anomalies, and fleet movements that might fool a single station.
properties.body: Veld Spire is a pulsar-based early warning system of shielded towers, observation platforms, and high-sensitivity signal stations. Its crews watch for pirates, Warp Beasts, lane anomalies, and unauthorized fleet movement.
properties.tags_text: Veld Spire System Bastrel Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Veld Spire System

properties.raw_text:
Deyros System
Sector Name: Deyros Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Deyros is a dry frontier prefecture where Crown law thins into practical violence. Walled settlements, armed convoys, and fortified civic compounds define normal life.
properties.description: The Deyros system is a dry frontier prefecture spread across inhabited Deyros, the industrial worlds Copper Writ and Prefect’s Reach, Dustwake, Magistrate, and its convoy moon. Crown law weakens with distance from the main world, leaving walled settlements, armed transports, fortified compounds, and local violence to hold the routes together.
properties.body: Deyros is a dry frontier prefecture where Crown law thins into practical violence. Walled settlements, armed convoys, and fortified civic compounds define normal life.
properties.tags_text: Deyros System Deyros Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Deyros System

properties.raw_text:
Harrier Field System
Sector Name: Deyros Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant
Description: Harrier Field is a corsair interdiction base hidden among dense asteroid fields. Patrol craft, pre-mined ambush lanes, and displayed pirate hulks make it the Crown’s forward answer to Orak raiding.
properties.description: Harrier Field is a corsair-interdiction system built around a dense asteroid belt, the hidden Harrier Field Base, three supporting planets, and pre-mined approach lanes. Patrol craft use the field’s rocks, sensor shadows, and displayed pirate hulks to turn the entire system into an ambush ground against Orak raiders.
properties.body: Harrier Field is a corsair interdiction base hidden among dense asteroid fields. Patrol craft, pre-mined ambush lanes, and displayed pirate hulks make it the Crown’s forward answer to Orak raiding.
properties.tags_text: Harrier Field System Deyros Sector Crown Concordant system sector glossary
properties.name: Harrier Field System

properties.raw_text:
Black Runnel System
Sector Name: Deyros Sector
Faction: Crown Concordant, contested illicit corridor
Description: Black Runnel is a black hole smuggler thoroughfare used to move goods through lensing shadows and unstable micro-lanes. The Guild advises licensed pilots that legal navigation standards do not apply inside criminal geometry.
properties.description: Black Runnel is a black-hole corridor of unstable micro-lanes, lensing shadows, hidden habitats, and the distributed anchor network used by smugglers to cross between Crown, Orak, and Karnate space. Legal navigation standards do not apply inside its shifting geometry, and no single station controls every route through it.
properties.body: Black Runnel is a black hole smuggler thoroughfare used to move goods through lensing shadows and unstable micro-lanes. The Guild advises licensed pilots that legal navigation standards do not apply inside criminal geometry.
properties.tags_text: Black Runnel System Deyros Sector Crown Concordant, contested illicit corridor system sector glossary
properties.name: Black Runnel System

properties.raw_text:
Luthan Prime System
Sector Name: Luthan Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Luthan Prime is the League capital and a major Guild headquarters, where trade, law, and movement intersect. Mercenary contracts, faction embassies, Guild jobs, and commercial disputes all converge here.
properties.description: The Luthan Prime system combines the League capital with Guild Central, the Exchange Ring, Mandate Court, embassy orbitals, industrial Merovar, oceanic Edrine, and guarded reserves around Covenant. Trade, law, diplomacy, and mercenary work converge across the entire system, making even routine transit part of a larger commercial proceeding.
properties.body: Luthan Prime is the League capital and a major Guild headquarters, where trade, law, and movement intersect. Mercenary contracts, faction embassies, Guild jobs, and commercial disputes all converge here.
properties.tags_text: Luthan Prime System Luthan Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Luthan Prime System

properties.raw_text:
Feyros System
Sector Name: Luthan Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Feyros is a polished finance and exchange world where convoy futures, war-risk indexes, noble scandals, and mech-part markets are traded in public. Pilots may find work here easily, but every contract has a beneficiary beyond the mission.
properties.description: Feyros is a finance system of exchange cities, reserve vaults, investment worlds, industrial Specula, the gas giant Othyr, and private negotiation sites on Ilyra. Convoy futures, war-risk indexes, noble scandals, and mech-part markets are traded throughout its orbital network, so every contract has beneficiaries well beyond the world where it is signed.
properties.body: Feyros is a polished finance and exchange world where convoy futures, war-risk indexes, noble scandals, and mech-part markets are traded in public. Pilots may find work here easily, but every contract has a beneficiary beyond the mission.
properties.tags_text: Feyros System Luthan Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Feyros System

properties.raw_text:
Miridal System
Sector Name: Luthan Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Miridal is an academy and culture world where universities function as corporations and museums double as patent vaults. It trains negotiators, engineers, economists, artists, and information brokers for League service.
properties.description: The Miridal system is a corporate academic network spanning Miridal and its moons, Cadence, Ortheon, Avenor, the Aeon Archive, the Patent Vault, and research sites around Lysara. Universities, performance cities, museums, and laboratories compete across the system to train League specialists and secure ownership of anything they discover.
properties.body: Miridal is an academy and culture world where universities function as corporations and museums double as patent vaults. It trains negotiators, engineers, economists, artists, and information brokers for League service.
properties.tags_text: Miridal System Luthan Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Miridal System

properties.raw_text:
Ferrith Hub System
Sector Name: Ferrith Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Ferrith Hub is an asteroid bazaar welded into a brown-dwarf system, full of auction bays, chop shops, private armies, religious booths, and variable gravity corridors. Anything can be bought here, including things that should not exist.
properties.description: Ferrith Hub is a brown-dwarf market system spread across a central asteroid bazaar and the mining clusters Veyr’s Folly, Nacre Reach, Saint Orro Claim, Serrake Claim, and Hollow Crown Claim. Auction bays, chop shops, gun markets, private armies, and religious booths move between rocks and docks, allowing almost anything to be bought somewhere in the system.
properties.body: Ferrith Hub is an asteroid bazaar welded into a brown-dwarf system, full of auction bays, chop shops, private armies, religious booths, and variable gravity corridors. Anything can be bought here, including things that should not exist.
properties.tags_text: Ferrith Hub System Ferrith Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Ferrith Hub System

properties.raw_text:
Rynnel System
Sector Name: Ferrith Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Rynnel is the League’s luxury manufacturing system, known for ocean resorts, orbital casinos, designer gene-spas, and high-status goods. Its beauty is profitable, controlled, and built above service warrens.
properties.description: Rynnel is a luxury system built around the ocean world Rynnel, its resort moons, the Crown of Chance habitat, the Velvet Exchange, industrial Vitrine, and the scenic gas giant Radiance. Its beauty, gene-spas, casinos, and designer goods depend on a system-wide service economy hidden behind controlled climates and exclusive transit lanes.
properties.body: Rynnel is the League’s luxury manufacturing system, known for ocean resorts, orbital casinos, designer gene-spas, and high-status goods. Its beauty is profitable, controlled, and built above service warrens.
properties.tags_text: Rynnel System Ferrith Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Rynnel System

properties.raw_text:
Vask Gate System
Sector Name: Ferrith Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Vask Gate is a customs and tariffs system built from scanning hoops, tax courts, bonded warehouses, quarantine pens, and weapon-sealed inspection docks. Pilots should budget time for delay, inspection, and unofficial fees.
properties.description: Vask Gate is a binary customs system whose checkpoint megastation, industrial Othran, tariff moon Selcar, quarantine orbits, and holding lanes regulate entry into profitable League circulation. Scanning hoops and tax courts dominate the Gate itself, but delays, seizures, and unofficial fees can occur anywhere between the inner planet and Quarantine Reach.
properties.body: Vask Gate is a customs and tariffs system built from scanning hoops, tax courts, bonded warehouses, quarantine pens, and weapon-sealed inspection docks. Pilots should budget time for delay, inspection, and unofficial fees.
properties.tags_text: Vask Gate System Ferrith Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Vask Gate System

properties.raw_text:
Ceyra System
Sector Name: Ceyra Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Ceyra is an industrial sky of aerostat cities, refinery moons, and tethered gas-harvesting stations around blue-white star giants. It keeps ships moving, factories burning, and fuel markets unstable.
properties.description: Ceyra is an industrial sky system of three gas giants, six major moons, armored aerostat cities, habitat spindles, and harvest nets operating under a blue-white star. Fuel precursors, exotic compounds, labor disputes, and storm damage move constantly between Orvannis, Veydras, Thaumyr, and their support worlds, making the system a linked extraction economy rather than a single colony.
properties.body: Ceyra is an industrial sky of aerostat cities, refinery moons, and tethered gas-harvesting stations around blue-white star giants. It keeps ships moving, factories burning, and fuel markets unstable.
properties.tags_text: Ceyra System Ceyra Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Ceyra System

properties.raw_text:
Morn Halo System
Sector Name: Ceyra Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Morn Halo is a refinery ring around a gas giant, processing what Ceyra extracts and sending it down the Mawward March. Docking schedules here are treated like sacred texts by tanker captains and Guild inspectors alike.
properties.description: Morn Halo is a refinery system centered on the artificial ring around the gas giant Morn, supported by the industrial worlds Ashfall and Vellum and cold storage on Calyx and Far Halo. Tankers, refinery crews, radiation shifts, and tightly sequenced dock windows bind the whole system into the processing link between Ceyra and the Mawward March.
properties.body: Morn Halo is a refinery ring around a gas giant, processing what Ceyra extracts and sending it down the Mawward March. Docking schedules here are treated like sacred texts by tanker captains and Guild inspectors alike.
properties.tags_text: Morn Halo System Ceyra Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Morn Halo System

properties.raw_text:
Brindle Shoal System
Sector Name: Ceyra Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Brindle Shoal is a cold tanker-yard system of drifting ice, metallic planetoids, and hollow-rock shipyards. It turns scattered fuel logistics into organized convoy power.
properties.description: Brindle Shoal is a cold logistics system of ice-and-metal asteroids, hollow-rock yards, the world Brindle, the outer ice world Wake, and convoy infrastructure at the Main Yard, Kestrel Yard, and Durn Haven. Tankers are built, crewed, fueled, and assembled across the shoal before departing in guarded formations.
properties.body: Brindle Shoal is a cold tanker-yard system of drifting ice, metallic planetoids, and hollow-rock shipyards. It turns scattered fuel logistics into organized convoy power.
properties.tags_text: Brindle Shoal System Ceyra Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Brindle Shoal System

properties.raw_text:
New Ulbar System
Sector Name: Ceyra Sector
Faction: Luthan League, deniable smuggler presence
Description: New Ulbar is officially marginal and unofficially essential, a smuggler colony where merchants, Orak captains, and compromised inspectors all pretend not to recognize one another. Purchases made here usually create leverage.
properties.description: New Ulbar is a dim brown-dwarf smuggling system consisting of the rocky world Ulbar, its tidally locked colony moon, the hidden Ulbar Cradle, and a debris field engineered to confuse sensors. League merchants, Orak captains, and compromised inspectors use separate approaches and shared deniability, ensuring that no transaction belongs to only one settlement.
properties.body: New Ulbar is officially marginal and unofficially essential, a smuggler colony where merchants, Orak captains, and compromised inspectors all pretend not to recognize one another. Purchases made here usually create leverage.
properties.tags_text: New Ulbar System Ceyra Sector Luthan League, deniable smuggler presence system sector glossary
properties.name: New Ulbar System

properties.raw_text:
Meyric System
Sector Name: Meyric Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Meyric is an exotic-mineral world rich in resonance-reactive materials, superconductive veins, and unstable substances. It feeds experimental weapons, Core interface technology, and high-end mech components.
properties.description: Meyric is an exotic-material system spanning the mineral world Meyric, its mining and administrative moons, the Crystech Vault, Veyrworks, the ocean world Neralis, the boomtown world Kharad, and outer prospecting sites. Resonance-reactive ores and unstable substances pass through several claims, courts, and processing facilities before becoming experimental weapons or Core-interface components.
properties.body: Meyric is an exotic-mineral world rich in resonance-reactive materials, superconductive veins, and unstable substances. It feeds experimental weapons, Core interface technology, and high-end mech components.
properties.tags_text: Meyric System Meyric Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Meyric System

properties.raw_text:
Osmir Reef System
Sector Name: Meyric Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Osmir Reef is a brown-dwarf asteroid system where rare metals grow in branching formations through broken planetoids. Miners cut the reefs delicately, since blasting them often destroys the value or awakens worse problems.
properties.description: Osmir Reef is a brown-dwarf mining system divided among the Inner, Crown, and Outer reef belts, with habitats and cutting stations embedded among branching metallic formations. Claim law and delicate extraction practices govern movement between Arken Spindle, Veyla Habitat, Keldran Cut, and Nareth Cradle because careless blasting can destroy both the ore and whatever structure produced it.
properties.body: Osmir Reef is a brown-dwarf asteroid system where rare metals grow in branching formations through broken planetoids. Miners cut the reefs delicately, since blasting them often destroys the value or awakens worse problems.
properties.tags_text: Osmir Reef System Meyric Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Osmir Reef System

properties.raw_text:
Glass Docks System
Sector Name: Meyric Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Glass Docks is a free-trade port famous for translucent orbital structures and visible negotiation chambers. The architecture implies honesty, but the Guild makes no such guarantee.
properties.description: Glass Docks is a free-trade system linking the ocean world Calyx and its moon Argent to the translucent dock complex, the industrial world Vitrion, and the inner world Prism. Negotiations occur in visible chambers and ships berth in clear structures under the primary’s glare, but the system’s openness is architectural rather than ethical.
properties.body: Glass Docks is a free-trade port famous for translucent orbital structures and visible negotiation chambers. The architecture implies honesty, but the Guild makes no such guarantee.
properties.tags_text: Glass Docks System Meyric Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Glass Docks System

properties.raw_text:
Tovresh System
Sector Name: Tovresh Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Tovresh is an escort-yard and convoy-marshaling system that keeps League trade from becoming pirate food. Its naval culture is orderly, disciplined, and built around protecting other people’s profit.
properties.description: Tovresh is an escort and convoy system spanning the inhabited naval world, its shipbuilding and training moons, the Escort Yards, Marshal Spine, industrial Drosen, provisioning waters on Aven Mare, and the outer anchor at Thalor. League trade survives because crews, supplies, escorts, and merchant formations are coordinated across the entire system before departure.
properties.body: Tovresh is an escort-yard and convoy-marshaling system that keeps League trade from becoming pirate food. Its naval culture is orderly, disciplined, and built around protecting other people’s profit.
properties.tags_text: Tovresh System Tovresh Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Tovresh System

properties.raw_text:
Waymaker System
Sector Name: Tovresh Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Waymaker is home to lane pilot guilds, route mathematicians, navigators, and families raised on dead-jump lore. Frontier routes that look survivable on a map often become suicide paths without Waymaker-trained pilots.
properties.description: Waymaker is a navigation system of calibration worlds, signal locks, the gas giant Ormec, the guild moon Waymaker, and the outer Rift Monitor at Sable Verge. Pilots and route mathematicians compare observations across these separated stations to maintain frontier paths that become lethal when reduced to a single chart.
properties.body: Waymaker is home to lane pilot guilds, route mathematicians, navigators, and families raised on dead-jump lore. Frontier routes that look survivable on a map often become suicide paths without Waymaker-trained pilots.
properties.tags_text: Waymaker System Tovresh Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Waymaker System

properties.raw_text:
Grey Twelve System
Sector Name: Tovresh Sector
Faction: Luthan League
Description: Grey Twelve is a bonded warehouse chain used for cargo, legal evidence, seized assets, war materiel, and containers too valuable to move quickly. It is boring in the way loaded guns are boring.
properties.description: Grey Twelve is a bonded-storage system split between Greyhold, its cryogenic moon, the gas giant Orsin, and twelve warehouse stations distributed between their orbits. Legal evidence, seized assets, war materiel, dormant estates, and restricted cargo are deliberately separated across the chain, making the system boring in the way a magazine full of loaded weapons is boring.
properties.body: Grey Twelve is a bonded warehouse chain used for cargo, legal evidence, seized assets, war materiel, and containers too valuable to move quickly. It is boring in the way loaded guns are boring.
properties.tags_text: Grey Twelve System Tovresh Sector Luthan League system sector glossary
properties.name: Grey Twelve System

properties.raw_text:
Dross Forge System
Sector Name: Karnate Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Dross Forge is the Karnate capital, a scorched furnace world under a swollen red giant. Its foundry-cities, labor warrens, oligarch towers, and mech trenches form the political and industrial heart of Karnate power.
properties.description: Dross Forge is the Karnate capital system beneath a swollen red giant, linking the furnace world to Vhalka, Orvek, Morvane, Kest Vorn, Serrak, the Ashen Table Citadel, and the Varkesh foundry lattice. Politics and production are distributed through mines, labor warrens, orbital forges, fortress-stacks, and fuel operations around Vel Marr, making the whole system an engine of coercive war industry.
properties.body: Dross Forge is the Karnate capital, a scorched furnace world under a swollen red giant. Its foundry-cities, labor warrens, oligarch towers, and mech trenches form the political and industrial heart of Karnate power.
properties.tags_text: Dross Forge System Karnate Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Dross Forge System

properties.raw_text:
Shivra Belt System
Sector Name: Karnate Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Shivra Belt is a beast-ridden mining system where valuable ore keeps extraction crews working despite constant Warp Beast losses. Pilots assigned here should expect supervisors to value quotas over survival.
properties.description: Shivra Belt is a mining system of armed ore rigs, beast-infested asteroids, the processing world Heshra, support facilities on Vardek, and convoy staging around Nhal. Warp Beasts can silence any cluster without warning, but Karnate supervisors keep the system working because every rig feeds the larger forge chain.
properties.body: Shivra Belt is a beast-ridden mining system where valuable ore keeps extraction crews working despite constant Warp Beast losses. Pilots assigned here should expect supervisors to value quotas over survival.
properties.tags_text: Shivra Belt System Karnate Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Shivra Belt System

properties.raw_text:
Roughline System
Sector Name: Karnate Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Roughline is a press-gang depot where debtors, criminals, refugees, and kidnapped spacers are converted into labor crews, penal units, or furnace infantry. Licensed intervention requires careful jurisdiction review.
properties.description: Roughline is a press-gang system built around the depot moon Roughline and the processing stations, holding blocks, buffer spires, and troop docks spread through Veldros’s orbit. Ships arrive carrying debtors, criminals, refugees, and abducted spacers, then depart through Harrow Six with labor gangs, penal crews, and furnace infantry.
properties.body: Roughline is a press-gang depot where debtors, criminals, refugees, and kidnapped spacers are converted into labor crews, penal units, or furnace infantry. Licensed intervention requires careful jurisdiction review.
properties.tags_text: Roughline System Karnate Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Roughline System

properties.raw_text:
Rathmar Prime System
Sector Name: Rathmar Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Rathmar Prime is a planetwide foundry, cleaner than Dross Forge but no less merciless. Its production cities are arranged like machine parts and output high-grade Karnate war material at massive scale.
properties.description: Rathmar Prime is an optimized foundry system whose capital world, industrial moon Ostryn, Varkesh Yards, Ordan Forge, Cindor, Malven, Tharsis, Kerric, and the fuel anchor Rath Vorn operate as one production chain. The system appears cleaner than Dross Forge because its cities, mines, coolant sources, and shipyards have been arranged with machine efficiency rather than mercy.
properties.body: Rathmar Prime is a planetwide foundry, cleaner than Dross Forge but no less merciless. Its production cities are arranged like machine parts and output high-grade Karnate war material at massive scale.
properties.tags_text: Rathmar Prime System Rathmar Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Rathmar Prime System

properties.raw_text:
Jethra Belt System
Sector Name: Rathmar Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Jethra Belt is an unstable mining arc where binary gravitational interference makes navigation and extraction hazardous. Its volatile materials are critical to heavy weapons and reactor systems.
properties.description: Jethra Belt is a circumbinary mining system where six major rock clusters drift through unstable gravitational interference between an orange dwarf and a brown dwarf. Volatile materials move from Kheris Arc, Vannor Reach, Black Vey, Mornshard, and the other rigs under constant correction from Jethra Monitor, while miners contend with routes and rocks that refuse to remain where charts place them.
properties.body: Jethra Belt is an unstable mining arc where binary gravitational interference makes navigation and extraction hazardous. Its volatile materials are critical to heavy weapons and reactor systems.
properties.tags_text: Jethra Belt System Rathmar Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Jethra Belt System

properties.raw_text:
Gallows Reach System
Sector Name: Rathmar Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Gallows Reach is a weapons proving range where the Karnate tests systems too dangerous to fire near anything valuable. Barren test worlds, cratered target cities, and observer bunkers define the local economy.
properties.description: Gallows Reach is a system-sized weapons range combining the cratered test world, three proving moons, the industrial support world Belthar, telemetry from Harrow Array, and long-range trials near Varkane. Target cities, bunker replicas, prisoner assignments, and disposable instrumentation are distributed across multiple bodies so that weapons capable of destroying one site can still be measured from another.
properties.body: Gallows Reach is a weapons proving range where the Karnate tests systems too dangerous to fire near anything valuable. Barren test worlds, cratered target cities, and observer bunkers define the local economy.
properties.tags_text: Gallows Reach System Rathmar Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Gallows Reach System

properties.raw_text:
Iron Gate System
Sector Name: Ironfall Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Iron Gate is a fortress-shipyard built for siege endurance and strategic fleet repair. Direct assault is strongly discouraged; its defense platforms, armored docks, and kill-zones are designed to punish heroic thinking.
properties.description: Iron Gate is a blue-star fortress-shipyard system centered on Vorn, its fortified moons, the Iron Gate bastion, Vorn Yards, and Blue Hammer Docks, with mining and training worlds spread through the surrounding orbits. Layered kill-zones, reserve fleets near Thren Vey, concealed hangars on Surn, and hardened industrial routes make the system capable of repairing fleets while under siege.
properties.body: Iron Gate is a fortress-shipyard built for siege endurance and strategic fleet repair. Direct assault is strongly discouraged; its defense platforms, armored docks, and kill-zones are designed to punish heroic thinking.
properties.tags_text: Iron Gate System Ironfall Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Iron Gate System

properties.raw_text:
Drossfall System
Sector Name: Ironfall Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Drossfall is a toxic test world with corrosive atmosphere, contaminated moons, and sealed bunker settlements. Karnate authorities use it for weapons, armor, and exposure trials without concern for habitability.
properties.description: Drossfall is a deliberately poisoned test system in which the corrosive inhabited world, two contaminated moons, underground bunker networks, Kelor Quarantine, and disposal orbits around Veyr Oss isolate hazardous trials from valuable Karnate territory. Personnel and equipment move through sealed crawlers, quarantine rings, and controlled descent corridors because contamination is treated as an operating cost rather than an emergency.
properties.body: Drossfall is a toxic test world with corrosive atmosphere, contaminated moons, and sealed bunker settlements. Karnate authorities use it for weapons, armor, and exposure trials without concern for habitability.
properties.tags_text: Drossfall System Ironfall Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Drossfall System

properties.raw_text:
Breakwater System
Sector Name: Ironfall Sector
Faction: Karnate
Description: Breakwater is a minefield anchorage protecting Ironfall’s strategic depth. Entry requires exact codes, and crews report that older mines sometimes wake without command.
properties.description: Breakwater is a defensive system built around the gas giant Kharon Vey, four armed moons, the Breakwater Anchorage, the Iron Halo minefield, Karth Signal Lock, and Voss Sentinel. Exact approach codes are required because safe corridors shift through chained wrecks, mines, hidden guns, and autonomous defenses that sometimes react without current orders.
properties.body: Breakwater is a minefield anchorage protecting Ironfall’s strategic depth. Entry requires exact codes, and crews report that older mines sometimes wake without command.
properties.tags_text: Breakwater System Ironfall Sector Karnate system sector glossary
properties.name: Breakwater System

properties.raw_text:
Orak Station System
Sector Name: Orak Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates
Description: Orak Station is a mercenary bazaar welded from old hulls, hollow asteroids, rotating markets, and armed docks. No syndicate owns all of it, which is the only reason all of them can use it.
properties.description: The Orak Station system sprawls from the Tin Teeth and the welded bazaar itself to the Boiler, its three salvage moons, and Widow’s Wake. Mercenaries, pirates, smugglers, and deserters move between Gun Row, Hookside, Gutterworks, Knucklebone, Platejack, and Cold Pocket under competing syndicate rules that never extend across the whole system.
properties.body: Orak Station is a mercenary bazaar welded from old hulls, hollow asteroids, rotating markets, and armed docks. No syndicate owns all of it, which is the only reason all of them can use it.
properties.tags_text: Orak Station System Orak Sector Orak Belt Syndicates system sector glossary
properties.name: Orak Station System

properties.raw_text:
Korran Drift System
Sector Name: Orak Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates
Description: Korran Drift is a nomad city-flotilla that changes position constantly around a red dwarf. Its people live in engine-warmed hulls, market barges, shrine tugs, and patched warships.
properties.description: Korran Drift is a moving system of city-ships, family hulls, market barges, shrine tugs, and armed escorts migrating between Cinderball, the Shove, and the cold refuge of Icehook. Its population lives aboard the flotilla rather than on either planet, and the system’s political geography changes whenever Big Sell, Saint’s Tug, Cradle Chain, or Red Maw alters course.
properties.body: Korran Drift is a nomad city-flotilla that changes position constantly around a red dwarf. Its people live in engine-warmed hulls, market barges, shrine tugs, and patched warships.
properties.tags_text: Korran Drift System Orak Sector Orak Belt Syndicates system sector glossary
properties.name: Korran Drift System

properties.raw_text:
Slate Cradle System
Sector Name: Orak Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates
Description: Slate Cradle is a salvage system where dead ships are stripped, welded, re-cored, and returned to service badly. It keeps Orak fleets alive despite the absence of formal industry.
properties.description: Slate Cradle is a salvage system of wreck belts, stripped worlds, improvised yards, and fuel routes around Slingshot. Ships are pulled from the Pickings, Deadwater, and Black Scatter into the Cradle, Core Cage, and Crooked Weld over Blackplate, then returned to service with parts scavenged from across the system.
properties.body: Slate Cradle is a salvage system where dead ships are stripped, welded, re-cored, and returned to service badly. It keeps Orak fleets alive despite the absence of formal industry.
properties.tags_text: Slate Cradle System Orak Sector Orak Belt Syndicates system sector glossary
properties.name: Slate Cradle System

properties.raw_text:
Driftheart System
Sector Name: Drail Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed control
Description: Driftheart is a hidden syndicate seat where pirate leaders meet under truce. Its halls are decorated with trophies, hostage banners, and old Guild warrants.
properties.description: Driftheart is a hidden syndicate system organized around Blackrock, the concealed station-city, Truce Hall, the hostage moon called the Leash, the armed moon Hammer, and the covert dock Backdoor. Pirate leaders meet under truce only because every faction has assets, guns, and hostages distributed throughout the system.
properties.body: Driftheart is a hidden syndicate seat where pirate leaders meet under truce. Its halls are decorated with trophies, hostage banners, and old Guild warrants.
properties.tags_text: Driftheart System Drail Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed control system sector glossary
properties.name: Driftheart System

properties.raw_text:
Haven Rock System
Sector Name: Drail Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, free-miner aligned
Description: Haven Rock is a free miners’ moot and one of the few Orak systems that feels nearly honest. It is armed, rough, and suspicious, but it represents frontier civil society rather than pure piracy.
properties.description: Haven Rock is a free-miner system spanning the settled mining world, Cinderhead, Pig Iron, Copperhead, the Free Moot, Claim Yard, and the outer moons around the Keg. Claims, marriages, convoy rules, and mutual defense are negotiated across these sites, making the system rough and heavily armed without reducing it to another pirate holding.
properties.body: Haven Rock is a free miners’ moot and one of the few Orak systems that feels nearly honest. It is armed, rough, and suspicious, but it represents frontier civil society rather than pure piracy.
properties.tags_text: Haven Rock System Drail Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, free-miner aligned system sector glossary
properties.name: Haven Rock System

properties.raw_text:
Pike Relay System
Sector Name: Drail Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, pirate picket network
Description: Pike Relay is an information ambush system that listens to lanes, spoofs signals, and warns raiders about convoy movement. The station may look dead, but dead stations do not usually know where your ship is going.
properties.description: Pike Relay is an information-ambush system whose hidden sensors and transmitters are distributed between Doorstop, Dead Ear, Tripwire, Blindside, the gas giant called the Blind, and Long Ear. The main relay appears abandoned because no single installation needs to remain active long enough to reveal the whole picket network.
properties.body: Pike Relay is an information ambush system that listens to lanes, spoofs signals, and warns raiders about convoy movement. The station may look dead, but dead stations do not usually know where your ship is going.
properties.tags_text: Pike Relay System Drail Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, pirate picket network system sector glossary
properties.name: Pike Relay System

properties.raw_text:
Greyspan Station System
Sector Name: Greyspan Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed pirate control
Description: Greyspan Station is a pirate citadel built in a neutron-star hazard zone where sane governments avoid fighting. It is a fortress city of syndicate banners, slave markets, repair docks, and trophy halls.
properties.description: Greyspan is a pirate fortress system embedded in a neutron-star hazard zone, with the citadel shielded behind the Carrion, Knucklerock, Shrapnel Sea, and the heavy guns of the Iron Smile. Chainmarket, Prize Row, repair docks, and syndicate districts make Greyspan Station the visible capital, but the surrounding debris and fortifications are what prevent sane governments from reaching it.
properties.body: Greyspan Station is a pirate citadel built in a neutron-star hazard zone where sane governments avoid fighting. It is a fortress city of syndicate banners, slave markets, repair docks, and trophy halls.
properties.tags_text: Greyspan Station System Greyspan Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed pirate control system sector glossary
properties.name: Greyspan Station System

properties.raw_text:
Spindle Rock System
Sector Name: Greyspan Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed control
Description: Spindle Rock is a vermin-ridden mining system where tunnels are sealed, reopened, lost, and sealed again. Miners carry bite alarms and emergency foam for micro-rifts.
properties.description: Spindle Rock is a vermin-infested mining system whose surface workings, the moons Bitepit and Leakside, Deep Cut, Red Seal, and Patchhouse are repeatedly opened, contaminated, and sealed. Ore still leaves the system because crews can abandon one mine or moon and shift extraction elsewhere before the Rift Vermin follow.
properties.body: Spindle Rock is a vermin-ridden mining system where tunnels are sealed, reopened, lost, and sealed again. Miners carry bite alarms and emergency foam for micro-rifts.
properties.tags_text: Spindle Rock System Greyspan Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed control system sector glossary
properties.name: Spindle Rock System

properties.raw_text:
Ash Rack System
Sector Name: Greyspan Sector
Faction: Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed salvage control
Description: Ash Rack is a burned outpost system valuable because of what happened there, not what remains. Scavengers strip it by day and avoid certain decks by night.
properties.description: Ash Rack is a burned system of ruined worlds, orbital shells, salvage camps, and debris whose value lies in evidence of the disaster that destroyed it. Charhouse, Crooked Halo, Deadlight, Burnscar, Sootball, and the Peel are stripped independently by scavengers who agree only that certain decks and signals should be avoided.
properties.body: Ash Rack is a burned outpost system valuable because of what happened there, not what remains. Scavengers strip it by day and avoid certain decks by night.
properties.tags_text: Ash Rack System Greyspan Sector Orak Belt Syndicates, disputed salvage control system sector glossary
properties.name: Ash Rack System

properties.raw_text:
Cindral System
Sector Name: Cindral Sector
Faction: Cindral March
Description: Cindral is the March capital under the dying Ember Sun, a world of bronze skies, heat shielding, evacuation tunnels, and old fortress walls. Its people are fatalistic, disciplined, and stubborn enough to remain.
properties.description: The Cindral system orbits the dying Ember Sun and is planned around eventual failure, with the capital on Cindral-II, emergency government sites on its moon, the Cindral Bastion, Ember Refuge, industrial Cindral-III, and reserve settlements on Cindral-IV. Bronze skies and unstable seasons define the capital world, but evacuation routes, shelters, fuel stores, and continuity infrastructure extend across the entire system.
properties.body: Cindral is the March capital under the dying Ember Sun, a world of bronze skies, heat shielding, evacuation tunnels, and old fortress walls. Its people are fatalistic, disciplined, and stubborn enough to remain.
properties.tags_text: Cindral System Cindral Sector Cindral March system sector glossary
properties.name: Cindral System

properties.raw_text:
Braxis Hold System
Sector Name: Cindral Sector
Faction: Cindral March
Description: Braxis Hold is the March’s bastion system, a cold fortress world built to hold when Mawward threats push inward. Any defense here should be understood as buying time for someone else.
properties.description: Braxis Hold is the March’s layered bastion system, linking Braxis-III and its defense moons to the Long Wall, the orbital redoubt, the industrial world Braxis-II, the inhabited frontier on Braxis-IV, and outer warning positions around Braxis-V. Every body contributes guns, supplies, population, or warning time to a defense intended to delay Mawward threats long enough for the rest of the March to react.
properties.body: Braxis Hold is the March’s bastion system, a cold fortress world built to hold when Mawward threats push inward. Any defense here should be understood as buying time for someone else.
properties.tags_text: Braxis Hold System Cindral Sector Cindral March system sector glossary
properties.name: Braxis Hold System

properties.raw_text:
Red Choir System
Sector Name: Cindral Sector
Faction: Cindral March
Description: Red Choir is an evacuation rally point built around muster fields, refugee docks, triage ships, and emergency beacon towers. Its name comes from the alarm tones that echo when a colony is lost.
properties.description: Red Choir is an evacuation system centered on Red Choir-II and its refugee moon, with the rally station, Morrow Refuge, Kestrel Triage, Last Harbor, surface muster fields, and sealed stores operating as one emergency network. When a frontier colony falls, refugees are sorted, treated, housed, and assigned transport across the system while the alarm tones that gave Red Choir its name continue to sound.
properties.body: Red Choir is an evacuation rally point built around muster fields, refugee docks, triage ships, and emergency beacon towers. Its name comes from the alarm tones that echo when a colony is lost.
properties.tags_text: Red Choir System Cindral Sector Cindral March system sector glossary
properties.name: Red Choir System

properties.raw_text:
Ashveil Prime System
Sector Name: Ashveil Sector
Faction: Cindral March
Description: Ashveil Prime is a fading-star frontier capital of ash deserts, sealed towns, and permanent alert infrastructure. It links the Mawward March to the Silent Way and lives with one eye on the rim.
properties.description: Ashveil is a fading-star frontier system stretching from the observatories of Ashveil-I and the mines of Ashveil-II to the inhabited world Ashveil-III, the Command bastion, Cinder Relay, cold reserve worlds, and Red Lantern beyond Ashveil-VI. It connects the Mawward March to the Silent Way through a chain of bunkers, relays, listening posts, and fallback settlements rather than through one capital alone.
properties.body: Ashveil Prime is a fading-star frontier capital of ash deserts, sealed towns, and permanent alert infrastructure. It links the Mawward March to the Silent Way and lives with one eye on the rim.
properties.tags_text: Ashveil Prime System Ashveil Sector Cindral March system sector glossary
properties.name: Ashveil Prime System

properties.raw_text:
Duskwatch System
Sector Name: Ashveil Sector
Faction: Cindral March
Description: Duskwatch is a permanent alert post around a brown dwarf, built to monitor Maw activity, deep-space distortions, and lane instability. If its alarms sound, pilots should assume the first report is incomplete.
properties.description: Duskwatch is a dark brown-dwarf surveillance system consisting of the cold world Duskwatch-I, its artificial light mirrors, Duskwatch Post, and the separated Kest, Orra, and Veyr watch stations. Crews compare observations across the system because any single post may be deceived, compromised, or simply fail to remember what triggered an alert.
properties.body: Duskwatch is a permanent alert post around a brown dwarf, built to monitor Maw activity, deep-space distortions, and lane instability. If its alarms sound, pilots should assume the first report is incomplete.
properties.tags_text: Duskwatch System Ashveil Sector Cindral March system sector glossary
properties.name: Duskwatch System

properties.raw_text:
Hollow Verge System
Sector Name: Ashveil Sector
Faction: Cindral March, abandoned jurisdiction
Description: Hollow Verge is an abandoned rim system where ordered settlement gave way to loss. Empty habitats, cycling defense grids, and illegal squatters are all that remain of the old frontier claim.
properties.description: Hollow Verge is an abandoned frontier system of three former colony worlds, derelict stations, dead defense grids, salvage sites, and illegal habitation. Veyla Station, Morrow Spindle, Kestrel Relay, Sable Cradle, and Far Orison still cycle fragments of their old functions, leaving the system empty enough to be lawless but active enough to be dangerous.
properties.body: Hollow Verge is an abandoned rim system where ordered settlement gave way to loss. Empty habitats, cycling defense grids, and illegal squatters are all that remain of the old frontier claim.
properties.tags_text: Hollow Verge System Ashveil Sector Cindral March, abandoned jurisdiction system sector glossary
properties.name: Hollow Verge System

properties.raw_text:
Halix System
Sector Name: Halix Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Halix is the Compact capital, austere, controlled, and built around psychological defense. Its citizens report hallucinations, missing time, unusual dreams, and behavior that would be dismissed elsewhere.
properties.description: The Halix system is the Compact’s psychological-defense capital, linking the inhabited world to Morrow, Halix Command, Quiet Ward, the ocean world Vesper, industrial Locus, and the outer warning sites on Terminus and Pall. Civil calm drills and anomaly reporting begin on Halix, but the system’s separated stations and worlds exist to verify one another when perception, memory, or testimony becomes unreliable.
properties.body: Halix is the Compact capital, austere, controlled, and built around psychological defense. Its citizens report hallucinations, missing time, unusual dreams, and behavior that would be dismissed elsewhere.
properties.tags_text: Halix System Halix Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Halix System

properties.raw_text:
Crowthorne System
Sector Name: Halix Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Crowthorne is a Silent Way watch system of listening towers, sensor forests, and fortified settlements. Loudness is considered tactically foolish, and privacy is considered negotiable.
properties.description: Crowthorne is a Silent Way watch system spread across Briar, the scarred inhabited world, its gun moon Thorn, Crowthorne Watch, Hushline, the reserve world Vigil, and outer posts around Orison. Listening towers and sensor forests remain quiet by design, while separated crews compare dreams and observations to determine whether an anomaly is local or system-wide.
properties.body: Crowthorne is a Silent Way watch system of listening towers, sensor forests, and fortified settlements. Loudness is considered tactically foolish, and privacy is considered negotiable.
properties.tags_text: Crowthorne System Halix Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Crowthorne System

properties.raw_text:
Pale Hinge System
Sector Name: Halix Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Pale Hinge is a border-lock checkpoint designed to shut like a door if contamination spreads. Passing ships are screened for psychic irregularities, memory disruption, and unauthorized passengers.
properties.description: Pale Hinge is a binary border-lock system whose authority is distributed between Latch, Palisade, the quarantine moon Rime, the central lock, Memory Court, and Clean Passage. Ships are screened across several stages for psychic contamination, memory disruption, and unauthorized passengers so that the Silent Way can be sealed even if one checkpoint is compromised.
properties.body: Pale Hinge is a border-lock checkpoint designed to shut like a door if contamination spreads. Passing ships are screened for psychic irregularities, memory disruption, and unauthorized passengers.
properties.tags_text: Pale Hinge System Halix Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Pale Hinge System

properties.raw_text:
Nerith Hold System
Sector Name: Kryne Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Nerith Hold is an academy system where the Compact studies dreams as a battlefield. It trains monitors, psionic screeners, nightmare analysts, and pilots resistant to hallucination.
properties.description: Nerith Hold is an academy system rather than a single school, spanning Nerith, the training moon Somnus, two independent quarantine satellites, the Nerith Academy, and research or reserve worlds at Cipher, Lumen, Praxis, Lacuna, and Abyss. Monitors, screeners, analysts, and resistant pilots train across separate environments so that no one curriculum or shared dream can compromise the whole institution.
properties.body: Nerith Hold is an academy system where the Compact studies dreams as a battlefield. It trains monitors, psionic screeners, nightmare analysts, and pilots resistant to hallucination.
properties.tags_text: Nerith Hold System Kryne Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Nerith Hold System

properties.raw_text:
Kryne Relay System
Sector Name: Kryne Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Kryne Relay uses pulsar timing to detect impossible perception shifts, missing intervals, and dream-resonance anomalies. Its crews are brilliant, unstable, and heavily monitored.
properties.description: Kryne Relay is a pulsar-timed defense system built around the barren world Kryne and four shielded stations assigned to observation, communication, recovery, and anomaly analysis. The Compact compares each installation’s clocks, memories, and sensor logs against the pulsar.
properties.body: Kryne Relay uses pulsar timing to detect impossible perception shifts, missing intervals, and dream-resonance anomalies. Its crews are brilliant, unstable, and heavily monitored.
properties.tags_text: Kryne Relay System Kryne Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Kryne Relay System

properties.raw_text:
Blank Stair System
Sector Name: Kryne Sector
Faction: Halix Compact
Description: Blank Stair is a quarantine world for people, ships, and ideas suspected of carrying Mim influence. No one leaves without memory screening, and not everyone who passes is unchanged.
properties.description: Blank Stair is a quarantine system arranged in layers around Oblivion and its dead moon Null, with the orbital checkpoint and four sealed platforms separating suspect ships, prisoners, records, and communications. No person, vessel, or idea leaves through Last Seal without passing multiple memory and contamination reviews conducted by authorities that do not fully trust one another.
properties.body: Blank Stair is a quarantine world for people, ships, and ideas suspected of carrying Mim influence. No one leaves without memory screening, and not everyone who passes is unchanged.
properties.tags_text: Blank Stair System Kryne Sector Halix Compact system sector glossary
properties.name: Blank Stair System

properties.raw_text:
Veyra System
Sector Name: Veyran Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Veyra is the Choir’s pilgrimage world, filled with resonant basilicas, shrine-cities, public chant plazas, and old Core miracles. Commerce, law, and daily life are all shaped by liturgy.
properties.description: The Veyra system is the spiritual heart of the Choir Path, joining the pilgrimage world and its moons to the Great Chorus Conclave, Pilgrim Crown, Sariel’s monastery seas, the quiet worlds Eiren and Oriel, and outer shrines around Threnody and Halcyon. Pilgrimage, commerce, law, and fleet gatherings move through the whole system in processions rather than remaining confined to Veyra’s basilicas.
properties.body: Veyra is the Choir’s pilgrimage world, filled with resonant basilicas, shrine-cities, public chant plazas, and old Core miracles. Commerce, law, and daily life are all shaped by liturgy.
properties.tags_text: Veyra System Veyran Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Veyra System

properties.raw_text:
Low Cantor System
Sector Name: Veyran Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Low Cantor is a monastic shipyard system where vessels are built as prayers as much as machines. Hull ribs carry hymn-script, and shipwrights treat each launch as a future reliquary.
properties.description: Low Cantor is a monastic shipbuilding system centered on the yards and Hymnforge above Canticle, with cloistered work crews on Psalm, older settlements on Vestry, and fleet assembly around Benediction and Votive. Vessels are built, blessed, crewed, and joined to migratory processions across the system, making every launch both an industrial act and a religious departure.
properties.body: Low Cantor is a monastic shipyard system where vessels are built as prayers as much as machines. Hull ribs carry hymn-script, and shipwrights treat each launch as a future reliquary.
properties.tags_text: Low Cantor System Veyran Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Low Cantor System

properties.raw_text:
Hush Gate System
Sector Name: Veyran Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Hush Gate is a penitents’ port in a young, dust-rich star system. Pilgrims, criminals, exiles, and zealots pass through vows of silence or service before entering deeper Choir space.
properties.description: Hush Gate is a young, unstable system of dust, radiation, shifting debris, the worlds Penance and Exile, the penitents’ port, the Unspoken Court, and the Mute Lantern navigation station. Pilgrims, criminals, exiles, and zealots are screened and assigned vows across several sites before they are allowed to continue deeper into Choir space.
properties.body: Hush Gate is a penitents’ port in a young, dust-rich star system. Pilgrims, criminals, exiles, and zealots pass through vows of silence or service before entering deeper Choir space.
properties.tags_text: Hush Gate System Veyran Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Hush Gate System

properties.raw_text:
Choralith System
Sector Name: Choral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Choralith is an asteroid cathedral, hollowed into impossible chapels, low-gravity sanctums, plasma-lit nave tunnels, and sealed crypts. The Guild recognizes it as a protected religious site and advises pilots to respect restricted chambers.
properties.description: Choralith is a sacred asteroid system whose cathedral, three shrine rocks, internal anchorage, and distant ice world form a dispersed pilgrimage complex under a red dwarf. The cathedral is the largest site, but worship, silence, docking, and custodial life extend between multiple rocks whose impossible acoustics sometimes carry sound farther than their orbits should allow.
properties.body: Choralith is an asteroid cathedral, hollowed into impossible chapels, low-gravity sanctums, plasma-lit nave tunnels, and sealed crypts. The Guild recognizes it as a protected religious site and advises pilots to respect restricted chambers.
properties.tags_text: Choralith System Choral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Choralith System

properties.raw_text:
Songspire System
Sector Name: Choral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Songspire is a resonant Core-world where luminous seams rise through the crust and entire buildings are tuned around them. Pilots report stronger mech dreams here, which is not considered actionable without corroborating symptoms.
properties.description: Songspire is a resonant system in which the Core-world, its moons Cantor and Refrain, the Living Vault, Spireward, the ocean world Seraph, industrial Votive, and the outer moons of Orison all exhibit different relationships to local resonance. Pilots and cantors compare dreams, instruments, and environmental changes across the system because effects dismissed on one body may repeat on another.
properties.body: Songspire is a resonant Core-world where luminous seams rise through the crust and entire buildings are tuned around them. Pilots report stronger mech dreams here, which is not considered actionable without corroborating symptoms.
properties.tags_text: Songspire System Choral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Songspire System

properties.raw_text:
Miren System
Sector Name: Choral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir, compromised by Phage cult activity
Description: Miren is officially a troubled pilgrimage colony and unofficially a known spiritual infection point. Public shrines remain under watch, while sealed districts and undercities are subject to extreme caution advisories.
properties.description: Miren is a compromised pilgrimage system whose public shrines, infected districts, processional station, quarantine ring, frontier worlds, and isolated outer moon are divided between Choir authority and Phage cult influence. The danger is not confined to Miren itself, since pilgrims, refugees, sealed cargo, and suspected cult networks move between Mercy, Sable, Vigil, Miren’s Wake, and Absolution.
properties.body: Miren is officially a troubled pilgrimage colony and unofficially a known spiritual infection point. Public shrines remain under watch, while sealed districts and undercities are subject to extreme caution advisories.
properties.tags_text: Miren System Choral Sector Veyran Choir, compromised by Phage cult activity system sector glossary
properties.name: Miren System

properties.raw_text:
Evensong System
Sector Name: Choral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Evensong is a saint-mech crypt system where ancient frames are preserved as holy relics. Pilgrims come to touch cold armor and hear battles sung, but relic activation remains a Guild-reportable event.
properties.description: Evensong is a relic system built around the cold world Requiem, its crypt moon, the Evensong archive complex, and the First and Last Bell shrine stations. Pilgrims approach, enter, and depart through separate ritual sites so that any awakening among the saint-mechs can be observed from outside the crypt as well as within it.
properties.body: Evensong is a saint-mech crypt system where ancient frames are preserved as holy relics. Pilgrims come to touch cold armor and hear battles sung, but relic activation remains a Guild-reportable event.
properties.tags_text: Evensong System Choral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Evensong System

properties.raw_text:
Eyral System
Sector Name: Eyral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Eyral is a hollowed half-cathedral world, part planet and part sacred megastructure. Its inner caverns contain cities, sanctums, and resonant chambers beneath visible geometric scars.
properties.description: Eyral is a cathedral system whose half-hollowed inhabited world, construction moon Nave, monastic moon Sanctum, Pilgrim Crown, Inner Choir, and surrounding worlds preserve different stages of the Choir’s largest architectural act of faith. Visitors move between orbital reception, planetary caverns, oceanic Solace, quiet Vesper, and the auroral outer giant Magnificat rather than experiencing Eyral as a single monument.
properties.body: Eyral is a hollowed half-cathedral world, part planet and part sacred megastructure. Its inner caverns contain cities, sanctums, and resonant chambers beneath visible geometric scars.
properties.tags_text: Eyral System Eyral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Eyral System

properties.raw_text:
Veyth System
Sector Name: Eyral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Veyth is a chant-colony system where ordinary Choir life continues under red-dwarf light. It supplies population, food, training, and cultural continuity away from pilgrimage spectacle.
properties.description: Veyth is a colony system centered on the gas giant and its inhabited moons Hearth, Lesson, and Harvest, with ordinary settlements on Ember, Ashen, Quietus, and Wayfarer and regular fleet exchange at the Common Anchorage. Population, food, schooling, worship, and family movement are distributed across the system.
properties.body: Veyth is a chant-colony system where ordinary Choir life continues under red-dwarf light. It supplies population, food, training, and cultural continuity away from pilgrimage spectacle.
properties.tags_text: Veyth System Eyral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Veyth System

properties.raw_text:
Grey Orison System
Sector Name: Eyral Sector
Faction: Veyran Choir
Description: Grey Orison is a silenced shrine where singing is forbidden by local rite and Guild caution. Something made resonance dangerous here, and whatever remains is treated as a theological and operational hazard.
properties.description: Grey Orison is a silenced system consisting of two cold worlds, the forbidden shrine, Custodian’s Rest, two dead relays, and the Listening Post beneath a white dwarf. Singing and resonance devices are restricted throughout the system because the original event may have propagated through stations and signals rather than occurring at the shrine alone.
properties.body: Grey Orison is a silenced shrine where singing is forbidden by local rite and Guild caution. Something made resonance dangerous here, and whatever remains is treated as a theological and operational hazard.
properties.tags_text: Grey Orison System Eyral Sector Veyran Choir system sector glossary
properties.name: Grey Orison System

properties.raw_text:
Grailrunner
Visual: A Razor Skimmer rebuilt into a heraldic cavalry craft, its prow extended into a lance-shaped ram and its wing-vanes flared like a knight’s mantle. House colors trail from stabilizer fins, though most are burned ragged by engine wash.
Behavior: Shock cavalry skimmer. Trades some ambush utility for direct charge capability, using a forward resonance ram to punch through infantry blocks, light armor, and weakened mech joints. Often leads knightly assaults, opening a breach for Crown Lancers and Castellans.
Combat Report: “It crossed the field like a tournament charge. Then the prow hit the barricade and the whole wall folded inward.”

properties.raw_text:
Argent
Visual: A modified Ironclad. A polished white-gold tank with a shield-shaped prow, layered heraldic armor, and a turret framed by banner vanes and resonance emitters. The front hull glows with a faint protective field when advancing.
Behavior: Turns the Ironclad into a command and formation-anchor tank. It projects a directional barrier that protects infantry, retainers, and lighter vehicles pushing behind it, making it ideal for noble charges, urban advances, and formal battlefield breakthroughs.
Knight-Captain’s Log: “The Argent moved first. Every lesser machine formed behind their shields, and for six minutes the enemy could only shoot at a wall.”

properties.raw_text:
Typhon
Visual: A modified Basilisk. The barrel is thicker and heat constantly emanates from the end, covered in additional armor plating with an added crucible reactor attached to the barrel causing the vents to glow molten hot.
Behavior: Unleashes raw, condensed heat in focused beams, melting through armor and anything else in its path while carving continuous lances of destruction across fortifications and landscapes.
Surveillance Log: "The changes made appeared comical at first. We stopped laughing when it melted the bunker and everyone inside, then continued past it.”

properties.raw_text:
Slagmaw
Visual: A modified Ironclad. A furnace-bellied tank with a shovel-like prow, exposed smokestacks, and dense armor plates. Its tracks leave glowing scars in soft ground.
Behavior: Turns the Ironclad into a breaching and area-denial engine. It vomits molten slag into trenches, streets, and bunker mouths, making ground impassable behind its advance.
Foundry Marshal Note: “The Slagmaw does not conquer territory. No no no… it ‘preserves’ it until the Thralls can mine it out.”

properties.raw_text:
Emberwing
Visual: Modified Starhawk. A lean ace-pattern fighter with swept ember-black wings, reinforced VTOL thrusters under the fuselage, and heat-scored ground-attack rails beneath the cockpit. Its exhaust burns dull orange like a dying sun.
Behavior: Elite air-superiority fighter modified for vertical takeoff, low-atmosphere hovering, and brutal ground support. The Emberwing can duel enemy fighters, then drop into VTOL stance above a battlefield to rake armor columns with precision cannons and micro-missile salvos.
Pilot Log: “The enemy thought I overshot. I killed thrust, dropped nose-first between the buildings, and fired straight down the street.”

properties.raw_text:
Reliquary Mule
Visual: A white and brass armored transport with ritualistic glyphs on the interior, psionic ward plates along the exterior, and a roof-mounted harmonic emitter. Its ramp opens beneath a curtain of faint blue resonance light.
Behavior: A protected pilgrim and adept carrier designed for contaminated war zones. It shields passengers from psychic interference, steadies allied morale, and can serve as a mobile ritual anchor once deployed.
Pilgrim Account: “The screaming stopped when the doors closed. Outside, the battle kept going. Inside, the choir sang joyous hymns while we prepared for our next mission.”

properties.raw_text:
Canticle
Visual: A modified Basilisk. The barrel is surrounded by tuning rings, choir-crystal vanes, and suspended resonance chimes that vibrate before each shot. Hymn-script runs down the gun carriage in layered bands.
Behavior: Sonic resonance artillery. It does not rely only on explosive force. Instead, it finds harmonic weaknesses in walls, armor, shields, and tunnels, then shakes them apart from within. Its first shot marks the structure, analyzes the harmonics. Its second makes every crack sing.
Chantkeeper Record: “Steel does not resist if taught the correct note.”
